**Related:** Tax Calendar

---

#### Fiscal Year Variant (FY Variant)

A SAP parameter that defines how reporting periods within a fiscal year are structured for compliance reporting, unrelated to a company’s fiscal year definition.

**Also known as:** FY Variant

**Tags:** SAP Configuration, Tax Reporting, Fiscal Year

---

#### Time Unit (for offset calculation)

Defines the unit of time (daily, weekly, monthly, yearly) used to calculate the offset and subsequently the due date for tax report submissions.

**Tags:** SAP Reporting, Scheduling, Date Calculations

---

#### Ad Hoc Report

A nonperiodic report type in SAP compliance reporting without assigned periodicity, used for special or on-demand reporting requirements.

**Also known as:** Nonperiodic Report

**Tags:** Tax Reporting, SAP, Reporting Types

**Related:** Report Categories

---

#### Active-From Date and Active-To Date

Parameters defining the start and end dates during which a report category is active and capable of generating reporting tasks.

**Also known as:** From field, To field

**Tags:** SAP Reporting, Periodicity Parameters

---

#### Report Categories

Classifications assigned to reporting entities defining the specific type of compliance report needed, such as a country-specific VAT return.

**Also known as:** Reporting Types

**Tags:** Tax Reporting, SAP Configuration, Compliance

**Related:** Periodicity of Report Category, Ad Hoc Report

---

#### Customizing (in SAP)

The process of configuring SAP system settings and parameters, such as defining reporting entities and categories, to tailor the system to specific business or legal requirements.

**Also known as:** SAP Customizing, Configuration

**Tags:** SAP, System Configuration, Reporting Setup

---

#### Report RFASLM00

An SAP report that allows printing the EC Sales List using SAPscript or PDF forms, accessible via transaction S_ALR_87012400 or specific menu paths.

**Also known as:** EC Sales List Print Report RFASLM00

**Tags:** SAP, Financial Accounting, Reporting, EC Sales List, Print Control

**Related:** Report RFASLD20

---

#### Report RFASLD20

A standard SAP report used to generate the EC Sales List in digital formats such as list output or DME file, accessible via the accounting menu or transaction S_P00_07000221.

**Also known as:** EC Sales List Report RFASLD20

**Tags:** SAP, Financial Accounting, Reporting, EC Sales List

**Related:** SAP Report RFASLD20, Report RFASLM00

---

#### Making Tax Digital (MTD)

A UK government initiative promoting electronic submission and management of tax data through digital means, often integrated with SAP tax reporting functions.

**Also known as:** MTD Initiative

**Tags:** tax compliance, digital tax, UK tax law

**Related:** SAP S/4HANA Tax Reporting

---

#### SAP Report RFASLD20

A country-independent SAP report used to prepare EC Sales Lists in DTA format via the DME Engine, although support is being phased out in certain countries.

**Also known as:** RFASLD20, EC Sales List DTA Report

**Tags:** SAP reporting, EC Sales List, tax reporting

**Related:** Report RFASLD20

---

#### Report RFUMSV10

An SAP report providing additional lists for advance returns of tax on sales/purchases used primarily for reconciliation; it extracts data from BSEG but does not perform completeness checks.

**Also known as:** RFUMSV10, SAP Tax Reconciliation Report

**Tags:** SAP reporting, tax reconciliation, VAT reporting

**Related:** Tax Reporting Process

---

#### VAT Group

A grouping entity defined in SAP representing a tax group responsible for VAT reporting, to which controlling company codes and subsidiaries are assigned.

**Also known as:** Tax Group

**Tags:** SAP configuration, tax reporting group, VAT

**Related:** VAT Codes (Tax Codes)

---

#### VAT Codes (Tax Codes)

Specific codes assigned to transactions that represent different VAT rates or tax treatments, used to control posting behavior and map to specific VAT return form fields.

**Also known as:** Tax Codes, VAT Tax Codes

**Tags:** tax reporting, VAT, accounting codes

**Related:** VAT Group

---

#### Tax Reporting Date (BKPF-VATDATE)

An optional accounting document field representing the date relevant for VAT reporting, which can be activated in SAP to improve accuracy of tax return submissions.

**Also known as:** VAT Reporting Date

**Tags:** tax reporting, SAP customization, document date

**Related:** VAT Liability

---

#### SAP Report RFUMSV00

A standard SAP report used for generating VAT returns, selecting tax data from BSET table with filtering options; however, it is no longer supported in some countries.

**Also known as:** RFUMSV00, Standard SAP VAT Report

**Tags:** SAP reporting, VAT return, tax reporting

**Related:** Tax Reporting Process

---

#### SAP S/4HANA Tax Reporting

A functionality within SAP S/4HANA ERP system that enables management, generation, extraction, and submission of tax reporting data in legally required formats for various tax reporting obligations.

**Also known as:** Tax Reporting in SAP, SAP Tax Reporting

**Tags:** enterprise software, tax reporting, ERP systems

**Related:** Basic Compliance Reporting, Advanced Compliance Reporting (ACR), Making Tax Digital (MTD)

---

#### Co-sourcing

A hybrid model combining elements of insourcing and outsourcing where functional control remains with the in-house tax team, leveraging both internal and external resources to manage tax functions.

**Also known as:** Co-sourced Tax Function

**Tags:** tax management, service delivery model, hybrid sourcing

**Related:** Outsourcing in Tax Functions, Tax Process Solutions

---

#### Outsourcing in Tax Functions

A service delivery model where external providers perform tax-related services, allowing the purchasing company to pay based only on services rendered, reduce internal use of external knowledge, and minimize IT investments.

**Also known as:** Tax Outsourcing

**Tags:** tax management, service delivery model, business restructuring

**Related:** Co-sourcing, Tax Process Solutions

---

#### CbCR report

The summary document in CbCR that aggregates global financial, tax, and operational data of a multinational group for tax authorities.

**Tags:** tax documentation, reporting, CbCR

**Related:** Tax Reporting Process

---

#### Organization for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD)

An international organization developing policy frameworks such as BEPS to promote global tax standards and economic cooperation.

**Also known as:** OECD

**Tags:** international organization, tax policy

**Related:** SAF-T

---

#### Tax rate

The percentage at which tax is levied on taxable income, transactions, or assets, which can vary by tax type and jurisdiction.

**Tags:** taxation, direct tax, calculation, tax calculation, tax rules, tax, rate, percentage, Accounting

**Related:** Tax Determination

---

#### Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) standards

Industry-established protocols for electronic data exchange, allowing standardized communication between business operators and tax authorities in tax reporting models.

**Also known as:** EDI

**Tags:** data exchange, tax reporting, standards

**Related:** Centralized exchange model, SAP Interface to the Tax Authorities

---

#### Centralized exchange model

A tax reporting model where the tax authority manages a centralized regulated exchange platform that controls document format and data exchange methodology, including a tax portal exchange function.

**Tags:** taxation, tax reporting, data exchange

**Related:** Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) standards, SAP Interface to the Tax Authorities

---

#### Certified service providers

Third-party entities authorized to validate and exchange invoice data with tax authorities, enabling technical compliance and relieving government platforms from heavy technical burdens.

**Also known as:** Certified providers

**Tags:** tax reporting, service providers, data validation

**Related:** Standardization zone, SAP Interface to the Tax Authorities

---

#### Standardization zone

A conceptual area in tax reporting systems where invoices and data exchange processes are standardized by certified providers to ensure consistency and interoperability.

**Also known as:** Standardized zone

**Tags:** tax reporting, data exchange, standardization, CTC models, tax compliance, e-invoicing

**Related:** Certified service providers, SAP Interface to the Tax Authorities

---

#### Clearance model

A tax model where the tax authority performs a clearance function requiring tax administration approval before an invoice can be issued, without involving a standardization zone or intermediary service providers.

**Also known as:** Invoice clearance model

**Tags:** taxation, tax reporting, invoice management, tax authority functions

**Related:** Real-time invoice reporting model, SAP Interface to the Tax Authorities

---

#### Real-time invoice reporting model

A tax reporting model where invoice information is reported directly to tax authorities without exchange or forwarding by intermediaries, resulting in greater transparency but additional reporting burden on taxpayers.

**Also known as:** Real-time reporting model

**Tags:** taxation, tax reporting, invoice management

**Related:** Clearance model, SAP Interface to the Tax Authorities

---

#### Drop Shipping

A sales method where the seller accepts customer orders but the product is shipped directly from the supplier to the customer, impacting sales tax nexus and tax liability.

**Tags:** commerce, taxation, sales tax, logistics

**Related:** VAT Liability

---

#### Tax Gap

The difference between the amount of tax legally owed and the amount actually paid to tax authorities, influencing tax compliance requirements and reporting frequency.

**Tags:** taxation, compliance, tax administration, tax, measurement

**Related:** Tax Monitoring Process

---

#### Work Performance

A supply where the supplier processes or works on goods provided by the customer, then sells the processed goods back to that customer or another customer.

**Tags:** taxation, European Union, VAT, service, Project Management, Operations

**Related:** Normal Supply, Work Supply, Tooling (in Manufacturing Tax Context)

---

#### Work Supply

A supply where the supplier makes goods themselves, sourcing materials, and sells the finished product; the supplier is responsible both for the goods and their processing.

**Tags:** taxation, European Union, VAT, manufacturing, Project Management, Operations

**Related:** Work Performance

---

#### Normal Supply

A transaction selling goods as is, without additional work or processing by the supplier, e.g., a retailer selling a ready product to a customer.

**Also known as:** Standard Supply

**Tags:** taxation, European Union, VAT, commerce

**Related:** Work Performance

---

#### Intra-Community Supply of Manufactured Parts

The tax-exempt delivery of manufactured parts shipped between EU member states, typically considered an intra-community supply under the EU VAT Directive.

**Tags:** taxation, European Union, VAT, manufacturing, cross-border transactions

**Related:** Tax Exemption (Indirect Tax Context), Intra-Community Acquisition

---

#### Tooling (in Manufacturing Tax Context)

Special tools or molds created by suppliers to produce parts; from a tax perspective, tooling costs may have distinct treatment, especially in cross-border transactions where tools remain with suppliers and only final parts are delivered.

**Tags:** manufacturing, VAT, indirect tax, cross-border transactions

**Related:** Work Performance

---

#### Double Taxation (Indirect Tax Context)

The imposition of tax by multiple jurisdictions or more than once on the same transaction or income, often a risk in cross-border chain transactions.

**Tags:** taxation, indirect tax, VAT, sales tax

**Related:** Tax Exemption (Indirect Tax Context)

---

#### Resale Exemption Certificate

A document provided by a seller to a supplier in the U.S. that exempts the supplier from charging sales tax on goods purchased for resale.

**Also known as:** Resale Certificate

**Tags:** taxation, United States, sales tax, exemption

**Related:** Tax Exemption (Indirect Tax Context)

---

#### Intra-Community Acquisition

The purchase of goods by a business in one EU member state from a supplier in another, where VAT is accounted for in the destination country.

**Also known as:** EU Intra-Community Acquisition

**Tags:** taxation, European Union, VAT, cross-border transactions

**Related:** Intra-Community Supply of Manufactured Parts

---

#### Intra-Community Triangulation

A special VAT simplification mechanism within the EU that applies when three parties from three different member states engage in chain transactions, allowing the middle party to avoid VAT registration in the countries of the other parties.

**Also known as:** EU Triangulation Rule

**Tags:** taxation, VAT, European Union, chain transactions, simplification rules

**Related:** Intermediary Function (in Chain Transactions), Indirect tax return

---

#### Tax-Exempt Transaction

A transaction that is excluded by law or regulation from taxation, meaning no VAT or sales tax is charged or due.

**Also known as:** Non-Taxable Transaction

**Tags:** taxation, exemption, VAT

**Related:** Tax Exemption (Indirect Tax Context)

---

#### Taxable Transaction

A transaction that, according to tax laws or directives like the EU VAT directive, is subject to taxation such as VAT or sales tax.

**Also known as:** Taxable Supply, Taxable Event

**Tags:** taxation, economic transaction, VAT

---

#### EU VAT Directive

A regulatory framework established by the European Union that defines the place and conditions for taxable transactions involving value-added tax (VAT) within member states.

**Also known as:** European Union VAT Directive

**Tags:** taxation, European Union, VAT, regulation

---

#### VAT ID Number

A unique identifier assigned to taxable persons within VAT jurisdictions, often required for exemptions or special treatments in chain transactions and cross-border supplies.

**Also known as:** VAT Registration Number

**Tags:** tax administration, VAT, indirect taxation

**Related:** Tax Exemption (Indirect Tax Context)

---

#### Intermediary Function (in Chain Transactions)

The role of a dealer or intermediary in chain transactions who does not hold or stock the goods but arranges or facilitates their delivery between manufacturer and buyer.

**Tags:** chain transactions, tax law, indirect tax

**Related:** Intra-Community Triangulation, Tax Determination

---

#### Organizational Link (in Tax Grouping)

A criterion for tax grouping based on interdependence in organizational structures, such as shared management or personnel.

**Tags:** tax grouping, organizational structure, indirect taxation

---

#### Economic Link (in Tax Grouping)

A tax grouping criterion representing economic integration where related entities support or complement each other economically.

**Tags:** tax grouping, corporate economics, indirect taxation

---

#### Financial Link (in Tax Grouping)

A criterion for tax grouping where one legal entity has control over another through majority decision-making power.

**Tags:** tax grouping, corporate control, indirect taxation

---

#### Voluntary Grouping

A tax group option available in some jurisdictions allowing related entities to choose to be treated as one taxable entity for indirect tax purposes.

**Tags:** tax groups, indirect taxation, tax compliance

**Related:** Tax Group

---

#### Mandatory Grouping

A type of tax group arrangement required by law in some countries where certain related entities must be treated as a single taxable entity.

**Tags:** tax groups, indirect taxation, tax compliance

**Related:** Tax Group

---

#### EU VAT Directive (Article 137)

A provision allowing businesses within the EU to waive certain tax exemptions and opt to tax transactions to reclaim input VAT.

**Tags:** European Union law, indirect taxation, tax policy

---

#### Sale and Rental of Immovable Property Tax Exemption

The frequent exemption of indirect taxes on transactions involving immovable property or land supply under indirect tax regimes.

**Tags:** tax exemptions, real estate, indirect taxation

**Related:** Tax Exemption (Indirect Tax Context)

---

#### Financial, Insurance, and Postal Activities Tax Exemption

Exemptions in indirect tax laws for certain financial services, insurance, and postal services, often to promote public interest or avoid complex tax calculations.

**Tags:** tax exemptions, financial services, indirect taxation

**Related:** Tax Exemption (Indirect Tax Context)

---

#### Medical Care Tax Exemption

Indirect tax exemptions on medical services such as those by doctors, midwives, and dentists, and on delivery of blood and organs for health treatment purposes.

**Tags:** tax exemptions, healthcare, indirect taxation

**Related:** Tax Exemption (Indirect Tax Context)

---

#### Social Security Tax Exemption

Exemptions from indirect tax applied to payments or services related to social security, refugee care, community support, and aid for homeless persons.

**Tags:** tax exemptions, social policy, indirect taxation

**Related:** Tax Exemption (Indirect Tax Context)

---

#### Education Tax Exemption

The exemption of indirect taxes on educational services or institutions when meeting criteria such as official approval or minimum course duration.

**Tags:** tax exemptions, education, indirect taxation

**Related:** Tax Exemption (Indirect Tax Context)

---

#### Public Interest Tax Exemption

Tax exemptions granted for activities or supplies that serve public interest goals such as education, social security, and medical care under indirect tax laws.

**Tags:** tax exemptions, indirect taxation, public policy

**Related:** Tax Exemptions

---

#### Intrastate Acquisitions

Purchases of goods within the same customs territory or state that are taxable events but are not considered importations, as they do not cross customs borders.

**Tags:** indirect taxation, taxable events, domestic trade

**Related:** Taxable Event, Importation of Goods

---

#### Input Credit

The mechanism allowing a taxpayer to offset indirect tax paid on inputs against tax due on outputs; requires reversal if self-supply or gifts are not taxable.

**Also known as:** Input Tax Credit

**Tags:** indirect taxation, tax credits, tax administration

**Related:** Gift (in Tax Context), Input Tax

---

#### Gift (in Tax Context)

Goods supplied free of charge that may still be considered taxable supplies under indirect tax law, often subject to certain thresholds.

**Tags:** taxable events, indirect taxation

**Related:** Input Credit, Tax Determination

---

#### Neutrality of International Trade

The concept that indirect taxes should not distort international trade by favoring domestic over foreign producers, achieved by applying the destination principle for taxation.

**Also known as:** Trade Neutrality

**Tags:** international trade, tax policy, indirect taxation

**Related:** Indirect Tax Systems, Place of Taxation

---

#### Country of Destination

The country where goods are finally consumed, often used as the basis for indirect tax under the destination principle.

**Also known as:** Destination Country

**Tags:** international trade, taxation, indirect tax

**Related:** Place of Taxation

---

#### Place of Taxation

The geographic location where indirect taxes are applied on goods or services, typically either the country of origin or country of destination in international trade.

**Tags:** taxation, indirect tax, international trade

**Related:** Country of Destination, Neutrality of International Trade

---

#### Taxable Event

An occurrence defined by law that triggers the application of indirect taxes like VAT or GST, varying by jurisdiction and generally including supply of goods, supply of services, and importation of goods.

**Also known as:** Taxable Supply, Taxable Transaction

**Tags:** taxation, indirect tax, events, Tax Law, Tax Jurisdiction

**Related:** Taxable Events, Intrastate Acquisitions, Importation of Goods

---

#### Importation of Goods

The transfer of goods from outside to inside a country’s tax jurisdiction, typically involving crossing controlled borders with customs clearance, and constituting a taxable event.

**Also known as:** Goods Importation, Goods Import

**Tags:** indirect taxation, international trade, taxable events, Indirect Tax, Customs, Taxable Event

**Related:** Taxable Event, Intrastate Acquisitions

---

#### Tax Invoice (Brazil)

An invoice in Brazil that must be issued electronically in Portuguese and Brazilian Real (BRL), used for VAT purposes and presenting challenges for foreign companies operating globally.

**Also known as:** Brazil Tax Invoice

**Tags:** Brazil, Tax Documentation, VAT

**Related:** Brazilian VAT System

---

#### Gross-up Mechanism (Brazil Taxable Basis)

A method used in Brazil where the taxable basis for VAT includes the gross amount paid including tax, resulting in a higher VAT amount compared to the net basis used in standard VAT calculations.

**Also known as:** Grossed-up Tax Basis

**Tags:** Taxation, Brazil, VAT Calculation

**Related:** Brazilian VAT System

---

#### PIS/COFINS-Monofásico

A Brazilian taxation system where PIS/COFINS contribution is prepaid by the industry for the entire chain, disallowing later input tax credits for other chain participants.

**Also known as:** Monofásico PIS/COFINS

**Tags:** Brazil Tax, Indirect Tax, Tax Substitution

**Related:** Brazilian VAT System

---

#### ICMS-Substituição Tributária (ICMS-ST)

A Brazilian tax mechanism concentrating tax payment at the manufacturing or industry stage, requiring them to prepay ICMS for the entire production and distribution chain.

**Also known as:** ICMS-ST

**Tags:** Brazil Tax, Indirect Tax, Tax Substitution

**Related:** Brazilian VAT System

---

#### Imposto Sobre Serviços (ISS)

A municipal tax in Brazil on services, with rates determined by municipalities typically between 2% and 5%, and not part of the regular VAT system.

**Also known as:** ISS Tax

**Tags:** Brazil Tax, Service Tax, Indirect Tax

**Related:** Brazilian VAT System

---

#### Imposto sobre Circulação de Mercadorias e Serviços (ICMS)

A Brazilian state-level tax on the circulation of goods, transportation, and communication, with rates varying by state and federal regulations for interstate transactions.

**Also known as:** ICMS Tax

**Tags:** Brazil Tax, Indirect Tax, State Tax

**Related:** Brazilian VAT System

---

#### Imposto sobre Produtos Industrializados (IPI)

A Brazilian tax on manufacturing and importing industrial products with rates ranging from 0% to 300%, varying by product and government economic policies.

**Also known as:** IPI Tax

**Tags:** Brazil Tax, Indirect Tax, Manufacturing Tax

**Related:** Brazilian VAT System

---

#### Brazilian VAT System

A complex indirect tax system in Brazil consisting of multiple taxes on goods and services including IPI, ICMS, PIS/COFINS, ISS, and special substitution regimens.

**Also known as:** Brazil Indirect Tax System

**Tags:** Indirect Tax, VAT, Brazil, Tax Jurisdiction

**Related:** Imposto sobre Produtos Industrializados (IPI), Imposto sobre Circulação de Mercadorias e Serviços (ICMS), Tax Invoice (Brazil), ICMS-Substituição Tributária (ICMS-ST), PIS/COFINS-Monofásico, Gross-up Mechanism (Brazil Taxable Basis), Imposto Sobre Serviços (ISS)

---

#### Purchase Request

A formal document or process that initiates a purchasing cycle by requesting the procurement of goods or services.

---

Tax considerations related to grouping multiple entities for consolidated tax reporting or compliance purposes.

**Also known as:** Tax Grouping

**Tags:** taxation, compliance

**Related:** Tax Risks

---

#### Exemptions

Specific goods, services, or transactions that are excluded from tax obligations under law or regulation.

**Tags:** taxation, compliance

---

#### Taxable Events

Specific transactions or occurrences that trigger a tax liability under tax law, such as sales, purchases, or the transfer of goods.

**Also known as:** Tax Trigger Events

**Tags:** taxation, indirect tax, direct tax, tax event

**Related:** Taxable Event

---

#### Indirect Tax Systems

Governments’ frameworks to levy taxes on goods and services varying by jurisdiction, often involving multiple layers such as federal, state, and municipal taxes.

**Tags:** taxation, indirect tax, systems

**Related:** Neutrality of International Trade

---

#### Automation in Tax Processes

The use of technology solutions to streamline and improve efficiency and compliance in tax processes such as procure-to-pay and order-to-cash.

**Tags:** tax technology, automation

---

#### Tax Risks

Potential future exposures related to tax compliance, audits, or misstatements that can affect the financial position or tax liabilities.

**Tags:** tax management, risk management

**Related:** Tax Exemptions, Groupings

---

#### Account Assignment Type

Tax-relevant classification in procurement or finance indicating where costs or assets are assigned, such as cost centers, assets, or production orders.

---

#### Procurement Type

Classification of purchasing methods or categories, impacting tax-related accounting and reporting.

**Tags:** procurement, tax data

**Related:** Account Assignment Type, Tax Data Model

---

#### Sales Process Flow

The sequence of steps including contact inquiry, quotation, sales order, delivery transfer order, billing document, and accounts receivable in sales management.

**Tags:** sales, business process

**Related:** Order-to-Cash Scenario, Tax Process Solutions

---

#### Order-to-Cash Scenario

A set of business processes involving sales activities from customer contact to the collection of payment, impacting multiple tax types.

**Also known as:** O2C Process

**Tags:** business process, sales, finance

**Related:** Sales Process Flow, Tax Process Solutions

---

#### Procure-to-Pay Scenario

A business process encompassing purchasing from order request and execution to delivery receipt, invoice processing, and payment execution.

**Also known as:** P2P Process

**Tags:** business process, procurement, finance

**Related:** Supply Chain

---

#### E-Balance Taxonomy

An SAP implementation for electronic balance reporting to comply with statutory tax reporting requirements.

**Tags:** tax technology, SAP

**Related:** Direct Tax Lifecycle, Tax Reporting Process

---

#### VAT Deduction

The recovery mechanism that allows businesses to deduct the VAT paid on purchases from the VAT collected on sales.

**Tags:** taxation, indirect tax

**Related:** Value-added tax (VAT)

---

#### VAT (Value-Added Tax)

An indirect tax levied on the value added to goods and services at each stage of production or distribution.

**Also known as:** Value-Added Tax

**Tags:** taxation, indirect tax

**Related:** Indirect tax return

---

#### Direct Tax Lifecycle

The four-part process in managing direct taxes including tax reporting, tax declaration, tax audits, and management.

**Tags:** taxation, tax management

**Related:** E-Balance Taxonomy, Tax Monitoring Process

---

#### Buy-Sell Structure

A commercial arrangement where branches buy products and then sell them independently, often used to manage margins and tax risks across locations.

**Also known as:** Buy and Sell Arrangement

**Tags:** business model, tax planning

---

#### Low-Risk Distributor

An entity within a corporate structure that acts as a distributor with limited risk, often used in tax planning to limit high-profit margins and centralize tax control.

**Tags:** business model, tax planning

---

#### Operating Model

The framework or structure that defines how a company organizes its business processes, resources, and technology to achieve its goals, especially in tax planning.

**Tags:** business, tax planning, organizational model

---

#### Sales and Service Tax (SST) - Malaysia

A single-layer indirect tax system reinstated in Malaysia in 2018, replacing the earlier GST with sales tax imposed atypically at the manufacturer level rather than on the final consumer.

**Also known as:** Malaysia SST

**Tags:** tax, indirect tax, Malaysia

**Related:** Value-added tax (VAT)

---

#### Level of taxing authorities (US sales tax)

The hierarchical structure of government entities (state, municipality, city) in the US that impose and collect sales taxes, resulting in a complex multi-level tax system.

**Also known as:** Taxing authorities level

**Tags:** tax law, US sales tax, government

**Related:** Sales tax jurisdiction

---

#### Enterprise resource planning (ERP) tax determination

The process and complexity of integrating tax calculations, especially sales tax and VAT, into business ERP systems to ensure correct tax application across multiple jurisdictions and tax types.

**Also known as:** ERP tax automation

**Tags:** tax technology, business systems, tax compliance

---

#### India GST system

A unified tax regime introduced in 2017 in India replacing multiple indirect taxes including VAT, excise, and service taxes, consolidating them into a single Goods and Services Tax.

**Tags:** GST, tax reform, India

**Related:** Value-added tax (VAT)

---

#### Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) VAT system

A regional VAT framework implemented by GCC member states including Saudi Arabia, UAE, Oman, Qatar, Bahrain, and Kuwait, with varying VAT rates around 5%, except Saudi Arabia which increased to 15% in 2020.

**Also known as:** GCC VAT

**Tags:** tax, VAT, regional tax system

**Related:** Value-added tax (VAT)

---

#### Sales tax jurisdiction

A tax regime where sales tax is applied, typically at only one stage of the value chain, with liability rules and tax rates varying by geographic or governmental unit.

**Tags:** tax, sales tax, jurisdiction

**Related:** Level of taxing authorities (US sales tax), Use tax

---

#### Use tax

A tax complementing sales tax, applied to goods purchased in a lower-tax jurisdiction but used in a higher-tax jurisdiction, preventing tax avoidance by consumers.

**Tags:** tax, sales tax, indirect tax, Taxation, Indirect Taxes

**Related:** Sales tax jurisdiction

---

#### OECD International VAT/GST Guidelines

A set of international recommendations by the OECD defining VAT and GST principles, treating both taxes as embodiments of the same basic value-added tax features.

**Also known as:** OECD VAT/GST Guidelines 2017

**Tags:** tax policy, international guidelines, VAT, GST

**Related:** Value-added tax (VAT)

---

#### Intra-community supply (EU VAT)

The tax-exempt movement of goods between VAT-registered businesses in different EU member states, subject to the reverse charge mechanism.

**Also known as:** Intra-EU supply

**Tags:** VAT, international trade, EU law

---

#### Standard VAT rate

The minimum default VAT rate set by the EU VAT Directive at 15%, which member states must comply with unless reduced rates are applied under specific conditions.

**Also known as:** Minimum VAT rate

**Tags:** tax, VAT, EU law

**Related:** VAT Directive 2006/112/EC

---

#### Derogations (VAT Directive)

Allowances or exceptions permitted under the EU VAT Directive enabling member states to apply specific VAT rates or rules differing from the general regulations.

**Also known as:** Exceptions, Derogation provisions

**Tags:** tax law, EU VAT

**Related:** VAT Directive 2006/112/EC

---

#### VAT Directive 2006/112/EC

An EU Council Directive effective from 2007 harmonizing VAT systems in EU member states, establishing neutral competition by ensuring uniform VAT burdens regardless of value chain length.

**Also known as:** EU VAT Directive, Council Directive 2006/112/EC

**Tags:** tax, EU law, VAT, policy

**Related:** Standard VAT rate, Derogations (VAT Directive)

---

#### Staged collection process

The method in VAT systems where tax is collected at multiple points throughout the production and distribution chain, rather than only at the final sale.

**Also known as:** Multi-stage tax collection

**Tags:** tax, VAT, finance

**Related:** Value-added tax (VAT)

---

#### Taxable amount

The base value (usually the net amount) on which VAT or other indirect taxes are calculated, typically representing the full amount paid for goods or services before tax.

**Also known as:** Tax base

**Tags:** tax, finance

---

#### Non-standardization zone

The segment in CTC where invoices are exchanged directly between issuers and recipients without regulatory or standardization frameworks applied.

**Tags:** CTC models, e-invoicing

**Related:** Regulation zone

---

#### Regulation zone

The part of a Continuous Transaction Control system where tax administrations define data and procedural requirements aiming for common data standards.

**Tags:** CTC models, tax administration, digital tax reporting

**Related:** Non-standardization zone, Real-time reporting

---

#### Real-time reporting

The continuous or near-instantaneous submission of tax-related data by taxpayers to tax authorities to improve the timeliness and accuracy of tax administration.

**Also known as:** Continuous Tax Reporting

**Related:** Regulation zone

---

#### VAT Information Exchange System (VIES)

An EU system facilitating the exchange and validation of VAT registration numbers of taxable persons involved in intra-community transactions.

**Related:** Foreign VAT ID, Tax Monitoring Process

---

#### Business-to-government (B2G) e-invoicing

Obligation in many EU member states requiring public administrations to accept and taxable persons to issue structured e-invoices based on a European standard for public procurement.

**Tags:** e-invoicing, public administration, tax compliance

---

EU legal provision permitting member states to impose additional transaction-based reporting obligations to ensure VAT collection and prevent fraud without interfering with VAT principles.

---

#### European Commission (EC)

The executive institution of the EU responsible for proposing legislation and overseeing implementation of tax directives and digital reporting requirements.

---

#### Official correction form

A formal document issued by tax authorities to be used for amending previously submitted tax returns.

**Related:** Amendment of tax returns, Tax Reporting Process

---

#### Amendment of tax returns

The process of correcting or updating submitted tax returns due to errors, new information, or system mistakes, through formal or informal procedures.

**Also known as:** Tax return correction

**Tags:** tax compliance, tax filing, indirect tax

**Related:** Official correction form, Tax Reporting Process

---

#### Carryforward

The process of deferring a tax refund to the next tax period instead of receiving immediate repayment.

**Tags:** tax accounting, indirect tax

**Related:** Refund (in indirect tax return), Carrying Forward Statutory-to-Tax Adjustments

---

#### Refund (in indirect tax return)

A repayment from tax authorities when input tax exceeds output tax in an indirect tax return, subject to varying processes across countries.

**Tags:** tax refund, indirect tax, compliance

**Related:** Indirect tax return, Carryforward, Refund

---

#### Payment (in indirect tax return)

The amount due to tax authorities as declared in an indirect tax return, typically paid upon filing.

**Tags:** tax payment, indirect tax, compliance

**Related:** Indirect tax return

---

A periodic declaration submitted by taxpayers to report output and input taxes for indirect tax purposes, often leading to payments or refunds.

SAP standard functionality (e.g., Transaction FOTV) enabling direct electronic submission of tax reports like VAT returns to official tax authorities.

The communication mechanism or software connection used to submit tax data and filings electronically to government tax agencies.

#### Master Data Incompleteness

#### Indirect Tax Condition Type

**Related:** Group Condition Indicator, Manual Entries Indicator

---

#### Manual Entries Indicator

A setting indicating whether manual changes to a condition value are allowed; for tax condition types it is recommended to disallow manual processing to prevent errors.

**Also known as:** Manual Entries Setting

**Tags:** SAP, Pricing, Condition Types, Process Control

**Related:** Indirect Tax Condition Type

---

#### Group Condition Indicator

**Related:** Indirect Tax Condition Type

---

#### Round-Down Rounding

A rounding method where values are always rounded down to the next full number.

**Related:** Round-Up Rounding, Commercial Rounding

---

#### Round-Up Rounding

A rounding method where values are always rounded up to the next full number.

**Tags:** SAP, Pricing, Rounding Rules

**Related:** Round-Down Rounding, Commercial Rounding

---

#### Commercial Rounding

A rounding method where values below 5 are rounded down and values above 5 are rounded up to the nearest decimal or integer.

**Tags:** SAP, Pricing, Rounding Rules

**Related:** Round-Up Rounding, Round-Down Rounding

---

#### Customer Pricing Procedure

A pricing procedure assigned at the customer master data level affecting how pricing is determined for specific customers.

An SAP module that manages all processes related to order-to-cash, including sales order processing, shipping, billing, and tax determination.

**Tags:** SAP, Sales, Distribution, ERP Modules

**Related:** Material Tax Classification in Sales and Distribution

---

#### Pricing Exchange Rate Type

A configurable setting at the item level that determines how exchange rates are applied during data transmission from preceding documents (like sales orders) to billing documents, influencing pricing calculations.

**Also known as:** Exchange Rate Determination in Pricing

**Tags:** SAP SD, Pricing, Finance, Exchange Rate

**Related:** Pricing Date (Sales), ExRateType (Exchange Rate Type), Exchange Rate Differences, Copying Control for Billing Documents

---

#### Exchange Rate Differences

Gains or losses arising from currency conversion due to differing exchange rates applied between transaction and group/ledger currencies.

**Also known as:** Currency Translation Differences, Foreign Exchange Differences

**Tags:** Financial Accounting, Currency Exchange, Accounting Impact

**Related:** Pricing Exchange Rate Type

---

#### Alternative Local Currency for Advance Tax Return

An optional local currency maintained for advance returns on sales or purchases tax reporting, configured through activation and assignment to company code and reports.

**Also known as:** Alternative Local Currency, Advance Return Currency

**Tags:** Financial Accounting, Tax Reporting, Currency Management

**Related:** Tax Reporting Process

---

#### Copying Control for Billing Documents

SAP configuration determining how data, including exchange rates and pricing dates, is copied between sales and billing documents during document processing.

**Tags:** Sales and Distribution, Billing, Configuration

**Related:** Pricing Exchange Rate Type

---

#### Condition Type (Pricing Date Setting)

A sales pricing element in SAP where the pricing date can be set to various options like date of services rendered or billing date, affecting calculation and exchange rate application.

**Also known as:** Sales Condition Type Pricing Date

**Tags:** Sales and Distribution, Pricing, Configuration

---

#### Sales Document Types (Pricing Date)

Configurations in SAP that define how pricing and exchange rates are determined based on dates such as requested delivery date, valid-from date, or contract start within sales documents.

**Tags:** Sales and Distribution, Pricing Configuration

---

#### Tax Currency Translation Settings

SAP parameters that allow manual entry of exchange rates and change of the exchange rate date for tax-related currency translations in company codes.

**Also known as:** Tax Crcy Translation, Tax Exchange Rate Configuration

**Tags:** Financial Accounting, Taxation, Currency Translation

---

#### Activate Plants Abroad

A configuration step in SAP enabling management of multiple currencies and plants located outside the home country, used when more than one additional currency is relevant.

**Also known as:** Foreign Plants Activation

**Tags:** Financial Accounting, Configuration, Multi-currency

**Related:** Activation of Plants Abroad, SAP Note 1929128, SAP Note 1707438

---

#### Exchange Rate Upload Programs

SAP programs such as RFIMPNBS and RFTBFF00 used to automatically import exchange rates from external sources like XML files from the European Central Bank or market data services.

**Also known as:** Exchange Rate Import Programs

**Tags:** SAP Programs, Currency Management, Automation

**Related:** Exchange Rate Maintenance (OB08)

---

#### Direct Quotation

An exchange rate quotation method expressing the amount of local currency needed to buy one unit of the foreign currency.

**Also known as:** Direct Exchange Rate Quotation

**Tags:** Currency Exchange, Exchange Rates

**Related:** Indirect Quotation, Tax Determination

---

#### Indirect Quotation

An exchange rate quotation method expressing the amount of foreign currency needed to buy one unit of the local currency.

**Also known as:** Indirect Exchange Rate Quotation

**Tags:** Currency Exchange, Exchange Rates

**Related:** Direct Quotation, Tax Determination

---

#### Exchange Rate Maintenance (OB08)

The process and SAP transaction (OB08) used to enter, view, and manage currency exchange rates and their valid dates within the system.

**Also known as:** Exchange Rates Entry, Exchange Rate Setup

**Tags:** SAP, Transaction Code, Currency Management

**Related:** Exchange Rate Upload Programs

---

#### ExRateType (Exchange Rate Type)

A classification in SAP specifying the type of exchange rate to apply during currency translation, where 'M' is standard but custom types can be defined for legal or business requirements.

**Also known as:** Exchange Rate Classification

**Tags:** Currency Management, Financial Accounting

**Related:** Currency Configuration Fields, Exchange Rate Type Definition (OB07), Pricing Exchange Rate Type

---

#### Currency Configuration Fields

Key fields used to configure currency translations and exchange rates, including ExRateType (exchange rate type), Currency (currency code), Srce Crcy (source currency type), and TrsDte Typ (translation date type).

**Also known as:** Currency Setup Fields

**Tags:** SAP Configuration, Financial Accounting, Currency Translation

**Related:** ExRateType (Exchange Rate Type), Translation Date Type

---

#### Transaction OB22

SAP transaction code used to define settings for ledgers and currency types within the Financial Accounting Global Settings.

**Tags:** SAP, Transaction Code, Financial Accounting

**Related:** Currency Types

---

#### Global Company Currency

A currency assigned to a company or internal trading partner for standardized reporting and transactions within a global enterprise.

**Also known as:** Internal Trading Currency

**Tags:** Financial Accounting, Global Settings, Currency Management

**Related:** Currency Types

---

#### Index-based Currency

A currency type used in SAP to handle high inflation environments by indexing currency values to preserve financial accuracy.

**Also known as:** Inflation Currency

**Tags:** Financial Accounting, Currency Management, Inflation Adjustment

**Related:** Currency Types

---

#### Hard Currency

A currency assigned at the country level in SAP, often representing a stable foreign currency used for translation in high inflation environments.

**Also known as:** Stable Currency

**Tags:** Financial Accounting, Currency Types, Country Settings

**Related:** Currency Types

---

#### Group Currency

A secondary currency used in SAP assignments to consolidate financial statements across multiple company codes or legal entities.

**Also known as:** Group Currency Indicator

**Tags:** Financial Accounting, Currency Management, Consolidation, SAP, Currency, Finance

**Related:** Currency Types

---

#### Company Code Currency

The default currency assigned to a company code in SAP, used as the primary currency for financial postings and reporting.

**Also known as:** Local Currency

**Tags:** Financial Accounting, Company Code, Currency, SAP, Finance

**Related:** Currency Types

---

#### Currency Types

Classification of currencies within SAP Financial Accounting including company code currency, group currency, hard currency, index-based currency, and global company currency.

**Also known as:** Currency Categories

**Tags:** Financial Accounting, Currency Management

**Related:** Index-based Currency, Hard Currency, Global Company Currency, Group Currency, Company Code Currency, Third Local Currency, Transaction OB22

---

#### Third Local Currency

An additional local currency beyond company code and group currency, selectable within specific predefined currency types for ledger customization.

**Also known as:** Additional Local Currency

**Tags:** Financial Accounting, Currency Management, Ledger Configuration

**Related:** Currency Types

---

#### Activation of Plants Abroad

The process of enabling the Plants Abroad functionality in SAP via transaction SPRO under financial accounting global settings for tax on sales and purchases.

**Tags:** SAP, Configuration, Plants Abroad, Financial Accounting

**Related:** Activate Plants Abroad

---

#### Jurisdiction Codes

Codes used in some countries within SAP tax configurations to define tax jurisdictions for accurate tax calculation and reporting.

**Tags:** SAP, Taxation, Configuration

**Related:** Tax Determination

---

#### SAP Note 1929128

A document by SAP describing configuration details related to plants abroad and their unsuitability in countries with jurisdiction codes.

**Tags:** SAP Note, Plants Abroad, Taxation

**Related:** Activate Plants Abroad

---

#### SAP Note 1707438

A specific SAP guidance note recommending the creation of separate company codes for permanent establishments instead of using plants abroad when branches abroad are separate legal entities.

**Tags:** SAP Note, Organizational Structure, Financial Accounting

**Related:** Activate Plants Abroad

---

#### SAP Note

An official documented correction, explanation, or recommendation published by SAP to guide users on system behavior, updates, or configuration.

**Also known as:** SAP OSS Note

**Tags:** SAP, Documentation, Support

**Related:** Tax Determination

---

#### Reporting Country

In SAP Plants Abroad, a country to which tax codes and tax-relevant postings are assigned for VAT reporting and compliance purposes.

**Tags:** SAP, Taxation, Financial Accounting, Reporting, Country

---

#### Account Key NAV

A posting key in SAP used to post nondeductible VAT as a separate line item requiring a dedicated nondeductible VAT account.

**Tags:** SAP, Financial Accounting, VAT, Posting Key

---

#### Account Key NVV

A posting key in SAP used to automatically distribute nondeductible VAT amounts to relevant expense or revenue items without posting to a separate account.

**Tags:** SAP, Financial Accounting, VAT, Posting Key

---

#### Import VAT Account

A General Ledger account used to post import VAT, often related to customs processing and may integrate with SAP Global Trade Services.

**Also known as:** Import VAT

**Tags:** SAP, Financial Accounting, VAT, General Ledger, SAP GTS

**Related:** VAT Payable Account

---

#### Input VAT Account

A General Ledger account in SAP used to record deductible input VAT paid on purchases.

**Also known as:** Input VAT

**Tags:** SAP, Financial Accounting, VAT, General Ledger

**Related:** Nondeductible VAT Account

---

#### Output VAT Account

A General Ledger account used to record output VAT collected on sales transactions in SAP.

**Also known as:** Output VAT

**Tags:** SAP, Financial Accounting, VAT, General Ledger

**Related:** VAT Payable Account

---

#### Posting Without Tax Allowed Indicator

A setting in SAP that allows postings to a G/L account to occur with or without specifying a tax code.

**Also known as:** Posting without tax allowed

**Tags:** SAP, Taxation, Financial Accounting, Configuration

**Related:** Assign Country and Tax Code to G/L Accounts

---

#### Exemption Reason

A textual explanation printed on invoices to justify why no indirect tax is applied, often triggered by the tax code in compliance with jurisdictional regulations.

**Also known as:** Tax Exemption Explanation

**Tags:** SAP, Indirect Tax, Invoice Printing

**Related:** Certificate of Exemption, Nature Code

---

#### Nature Code

An alphanumerical code defined by tax authorities to categorize the nature of a transaction for real-time indirect tax reporting and exemption reasons.

**Also known as:** Transaction Nature Code

**Tags:** SAP, Indirect Tax Reporting, Tax Compliance

**Related:** Exemption Reason

---

#### Time-Dependent Taxes

A functionality in SAP S/4HANA that allows tax rates within a tax code to be changed in an audit-proof and history-proof manner, facilitating compliance with frequent tax rate updates.

**Also known as:** Tax Rate Validity Periods

**Tags:** SAP S/4HANA, Tax Code Design, Audit Compliance

---

#### Jurisdiction Indicators

Flags used in tax jurisdiction codes to determine whether taxes are calculated line-by-line or at the header level, and how discounts affect the tax base.

**Also known as:** Tax Jurisdiction Indicators

**Tags:** SAP, Indirect Tax Calculation, Tax Jurisdiction Codes

**Related:** Indirect Tax Rate

---

#### Country-Specific Withholding Tax Settings

Predefined withholding tax configurations in SAP tailored to the legal requirements of specific countries such as India, Brazil, Mexico, and others.

**Tags:** Taxation, SAP, Country-Specific Configuration

---

#### Certificate of Exemption

A document supporting an exemption reason for withholding tax, authorizing a reduction or elimination of withholding tax on certain transactions.

**Tags:** Taxation, Withholding Tax, Legal Document

**Related:** Exemption Reason

---

#### Exemption Reasons (Withholding Tax)

Reasons defined in SAP to exempt certain withholding tax transactions, such as certificates of exemption, which modify withholding tax calculations or reporting.

**Also known as:** WHT Exemption Reasons

**Tags:** Withholding Tax, Taxation, SAP Configuration

**Related:** Rule Set Mistakes

---

SAP-specific numerical identifiers that define the nature of accounting entries, such as 40 for debit and 50 for credit, used in posting withholding tax.

---

#### Account Assignment for Withholding Tax

The configuration of debit and credit general ledger accounts linked to withholding tax types and codes to enable proper posting of withholding tax transactions.

**Tags:** SAP, Accounting, Withholding Tax, Configuration

**Related:** Posting Keys (Debit/Credit Entries)

---

#### Company Code (SAP)

An organizational unit within SAP that represents an independent accounting entity for which financial statements can be created.

---

#### Condition Type (Taxation)

A configurable SAP element used in indirect tax and withholding tax procedures that represents specific tax conditions assigned to tax codes or WHT types.

---

A rule defining how withholding tax amounts are rounded during calculation to comply with legal or business requirements.

**Tags:** Withholding Tax, Tax Calculation, SAP Configuration

**Related:** Tax Determination

---

#### Accumulation Type (Withholding Tax)

A setting that determines whether withholding tax base amounts are calculated individually or accumulated over a period for calculation.

**Tags:** Withholding Tax, Tax Calculation

**Related:** Tax Determination

---

#### WHT Base Amount Calculation

The method to determine the base amount on which withholding tax is calculated, selectable between net amount or tax amount, also influenced by cash discount and accumulation settings.

**Tags:** Taxation, Withholding Tax, SAP Configuration

**Related:** Tax Determination

---

#### Withholding Tax Type (WHT Type)

Classification of withholding tax for different processing scenarios, such as payment posting or invoice posting, influencing WHT calculation and timing.

**Also known as:** WHT Type

**Tags:** Taxation, SAP, Withholding Tax

**Related:** Tax Determination

---

#### SPRO Transaction

SAP transaction used for system configuration, including maintaining customer tax classifications via paths like Sales and Distribution → Basic Functions → Taxes.

**Also known as:** SAP Project Reference Object

**Tags:** SAP, Configuration, System Navigation, system configuration, tax setup

---

#### Basic Withholding Tax

The original withholding tax method where WHT codes are created centrally per country and differ from indirect tax codes.

**Tags:** Taxation, SAP, Withholding Tax

---

Frameworks used to ensure comprehensive collection and management of tax requirements throughout the lifespan of a tax type within the implementation and operational business processes.

A subscription-based methodology offering predefined structures, processes, and integrated deployment to support customers transitioning to SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

A complete business process from initiation to completion that impacts tax compliance and reporting, such as record-to-report, order-to-cash, procure-to-pay, and plan-to-ship.

**Also known as:** E2E business process

**Tags:** Business Process, Tax Compliance

**Related:** Tax Processes

The end-to-end business and reporting processes that tax functions must manage and optimize to ensure tax compliance during system implementations and operations.

**Related:** End-to-End Business Process, Tax Compliance Management System

An SAP GUI transaction code used to extract, view, and analyze data from SAP tables, including those related to tax data.

The organized framework of master and transactional data within SAP systems used for tax purposes, including tables accessible via tools like transaction SE16 and Data Retention Tool (DART).

A classification grouping tax transactions by their nature or tax treatment, such as indirect taxes (VAT), direct tax nondeductible expenses, or withholding taxes.

**Also known as:** Tax transaction category

**Tags:** Tax Data, Tax Classification

**Related:** Tax Treatment

Stable data elements defining the company structure from an application perspective, such as controlling areas and company codes, which remain unchanged over time.

A financial data consolidation strategy where tax-relevant data is aggregated from multiple source systems into a centralized finance system for consistent reporting and analysis.

The technology foundation comprising systems, databases, tools, and data structures designed specifically to support tax operations and data management.

A viewpoint focusing on the requirements, impacts, and implications of tax laws and compliance within business processes and technology implementations.

**Tags:** tax, business

---

Also known as Industry 4.0 or the digital revolution, it refers to the current era characterized by a fusion of technologies blurring physical, digital, and biological spheres, impacting tax functions through digital transformation.

The electronic handling, validation, and archiving of business documents such as invoices and tax forms to meet regulatory requirements.

The arrangement of entities such as companies, cost centers, and profit centers in SAP S/4HANA to support tax reporting, calculation, and compliance.

Advanced Business Application Programming, a proprietary programming language created by SAP used for custom development and enhancements within SAP systems.

#### Tax Engines

Software components or external systems integrated with ERP platforms to calculate and manage tax obligations automatically.

**Tags:** tax technology, software

---

A conceptual framework defining how the tax function operates within an organization, including processes, roles, technology, and governance to achieve tax compliance and efficiency.

**Also known as:** Tax Operating Model

---

#### Tax exemption

A status or condition where certain goods, services, or entities are excluded from tax liability under specific rules.

---

#### Tax engine layers

The architectural divisions within a tax engine that separate different functions, such as rule management, calculation, and integration.

---

#### User exits (tax)

Predefined hooks that enable users to add or override standard tax processing logic to meet unique business needs.

**Tags:** tax processing, customization

---

#### Customer exits (tax)

Customizable points within tax determination to allow additional logic or rules for specific customer-related tax scenarios.

**Tags:** tax determination, customization

---

#### Tax deduction

An allowable reduction from gross income or tax liability brought about by qualifying expenses or provisions under tax law.

**Tags:** taxation, tax benefits

**Related:** Tax filing

---

#### Tax data recording

The process of capturing and storing tax-related transactional and master data for subsequent processing and compliance reporting.

**Tags:** tax data, data capture

---

#### Tax officer

An individual responsible for overseeing tax matters within an organization or government jurisdiction to ensure compliance and proper tax management.

**Tags:** tax administration, roles

---

#### Condition table (tax)

A structured table used to define specific conditions under which certain tax classifications or codes apply.

**Tags:** tax classification, data structures

---

#### Transaction codes in SAP relevant to third-party procurement

Specific SAP transaction codes used to manage sales and procurement documents in the third-party process, including VA01 for sales orders, ME21N for purchase orders, MIRO for invoice receipt, VL31N for inbound delivery, and VF01 for customer invoicing.

**Also known as:** SAP T-codes

**Tags:** SAP S/4HANA, Sales and Distribution, Materials Management, Invoice Processing

---

#### Residence of involved parties

The legal domicile or registered place of business of parties involved in a transaction, which can affect indirect tax determination especially when the exporting party differs from the supplier.

**Tags:** Taxation, Indirect Tax, Legal Jurisdiction

---

#### Incoterms (International Commercial Terms)

Standardized trade terms published by the International Chamber of Commerce that define responsibilities, costs, and risk transfer between buyers and sellers during international goods transport.

**Also known as:** International Commercial Terms

**Tags:** International Trade, Logistics, Indirect Tax

**Related:** Tax decisions

---

#### Invoice receipt

The process of entering and posting an invoice received from a vendor into SAP, usually referencing a purchase order to ensure consistency and tax correctness.

**Also known as:** Incoming invoice posting

**Tags:** SAP S/4HANA, Materials Management, Invoice Processing

**Related:** Digital Invoice Information

---

#### Item category determination

The automatic or manual assignment of an item category to sales order items in SAP based on material master data and item category groups, crucial for controlling sales document behavior.

**Tags:** SAP S/4HANA, Sales and Distribution, Configuration, Item Category

---

#### Triangulation simplification (ABC transactions) inside the EU

A special VAT simplification for triangular deals involving three entrepreneurs registered in different EU member states where goods are supplied directly from the first supplier to the last customer and the intermediary entrepreneur is exempt from VAT registration obligations.

**Also known as:** EU triangular deal, ABC transactions

**Tags:** Indirect Tax, EU VAT, International Trade, Tax Simplification

**Related:** Tax exemption

---

#### Place of Supply of Services

The geographic location, typically the customer's establishment, where services are considered supplied and taxed, differing from goods supply locations.

**Tags:** tax, services, geography, B2B

---

Legal regulations in some countries requiring invoices to be numbered consecutively and without gaps, often managed by SAP's Official Document Numbering (ODN) functionality.

**Tags:** billing, legal compliance, SAP

**Related:** Tax-Driven Invoice Requirements, Delivery-Based Invoice

---

#### Tax Identification Number (Tax ID or VAT ID)

A unique identifier for tax purposes assigned to partners, used in billing documents to enable proper tax reporting based on the tax destination country or partner role.

**Also known as:** VAT ID

**Tags:** tax, partner data, billing

**Related:** Output Tax Rate

---

#### Pos./Neg. Quantity

An indicator of the quantity amount to determine its effect on transactions; negative quantities reduce amounts, such as returns or order corrections.

**Also known as:** Positive/Negative Quantity

**Tags:** billing, quantity, sales order

**Related:** Billing Quantity

---

#### Delivery-Based Invoice

An invoice created from a delivery document, suitable for invoicing tangible products.

**Tags:** billing, delivery, invoice

**Related:** Sales Order-Based Invoice, Invoice Numbering

---

#### Sales Order-Based Invoice

An invoice generated directly from a sales order, typically for materials without delivery such as services.

**Tags:** billing, sales order, invoice

**Related:** Delivery-Based Invoice

---

#### VF Transactions

Transaction codes related to billing document processing in SAP, including VF01 (create), VF02 (change), and VF03 (display).

**Also known as:** Billing Document Transactions

**Tags:** SAP, transaction codes, billing

**Related:** Billing Quantity

---

#### Output Tax Rate

The percentage rate applied to the taxable sales amount to calculate the output tax for a transaction.

**Also known as:** Sales Tax Rate

**Tags:** tax, pricing, accounting

**Related:** Pricing Elements, Tax Identification Number (Tax ID or VAT ID)

Components of the pricing procedure for a sales item, including net price, output tax rate, and total amount.

Governance structures and procedures designed to monitor and control tax compliance risks within an organization.

Structured systems and processes implemented by organizations to ensure compliance with tax laws and regulations, often incorporating controls and risk management.

Activities within tax functions to verify accuracy and compliance of tax reporting and transaction processing.

Tasks within the tax function related to handling individual taxable events and ensuring appropriate tax treatment and reporting.

Regulations that require invoices to comply with tax authority rules for processing and clearance, impacting business and financial processes.

Processes in an organization related to managing finances, including treasury activities affected by tax reporting and compliance.

#### Value-Added Tax (VAT) Gap

The difference between the theoretically collectible VAT and the amount actually collected, indicating losses due to evasion, fraud, or inefficiencies.

**Also known as:** VAT Gap

**Related:** Tax Revenue, Tax Monitoring Process

#### Tax Revenue

The total income collected by governments through various forms of taxation.

**Also known as:** Government Tax Income

**Tags:** economics, public finance, taxation

The clear visibility and accessibility of tax-related data and activities to reduce tax evasion and enhance government revenue collection.

Activities and responsibilities within an organization related to tax compliance, reporting, planning, and strategy.

**Also known as:** Tax Department Roles, Tax Operations

#### provincial sales tax (PST)

A provincial-level indirect tax applied in Canadian provinces separately from GST and combined for the harmonized sales tax (HST).

**Also known as:** PST

**Tags:** taxation, indirect tax, Canada

**Related:** harmonized sales tax (HST), domestic indirect tax

---

#### harmonized sales tax (HST)

A combined provincial and federal sales tax system used in certain Canadian provinces, represented in SAP by a specific tax category combining GST and PST.

**Also known as:** HST

**Tags:** taxation, Canada, indirect tax

**Related:** provincial sales tax (PST), domestic indirect tax

---

#### domestic indirect tax

An indirect tax applied domestically within a country when no valid VAT ID is available for cross-border transactions, typically representing standard local VAT.

**Also known as:** domestic VAT

**Tags:** taxation, domestic trade

**Related:** harmonized sales tax (HST), provincial sales tax (PST)

---

#### SAP Note 91109

An SAP instruction note that modifies standard VAT ID validation behavior to allow the consideration of the VAT ID from the payer's country of residence if no VAT ID exists for the tax destination country.

**Tags:** SAP, tax configuration, software customization

---

#### VAT ID validation workflow

The system process for verifying VAT IDs during tax determination, which tests the VAT ID in the tax destination country first and then in the country of residence.

**Also known as:** VAT ID validation process

**Tags:** tax compliance, software workflow

---

#### country of residence

The country where the business partner (payer or sold-to party) is legally registered or residing, which can be different from the tax destination country.

**Tags:** taxation, business registration, international trade

---

#### Transaction OMKO

SAP transaction used for assigning tax indicators to account assignment categories, determining indirect tax treatment based on procurement reason.

**Tags:** SAP, transaction, tax configuration

---

#### Transaction OMKL

SAP transaction for maintaining tax indicators for account assignments, specifying indirect tax applicability per auxiliary account.

**Tags:** SAP, transaction, tax configuration

---

#### Transaction OMKN

SAP transaction used to assign defined tax indicators to specific plants, indicating their tax status.

**Tags:** SAP, transaction, tax configuration

---

#### Transaction OMKM

SAP transaction for setting tax indicators for plants, defining whether plants are taxable or tax exempt.

**Tags:** SAP, transaction, tax configuration

---

#### Plant (SAP)

An organizational unit in SAP Materials Management representing a physical location, relevant for tax indicator assignments and purchasing processes.

**Tags:** SAP, organizational unit, materials management

**Related:** Tax Indicator Plant

---

#### Embassy (Tax Exempt Customer)

A customer type usually exempt from VAT or similar indirect taxes, reflected in SAP by assigning a tax-exempt customer classification.

**Tags:** tax exemption, customer classification, VAT

**Related:** Liable for Taxes

---

#### Standard Product

A product with full or normal tax classification, typically subject to the regular tax rate during tax determination.

**Tags:** materials, taxation, product classification

**Related:** Tax Indicator for Account Assignment

---

#### Liable for Taxes

A tax classification indicating that a customer or entity is responsible for paying applicable taxes on transactions.

**Tags:** taxation, indirect tax, tax liability

**Related:** MWST, TTX1, Embassy (Tax Exempt Customer)

---

#### Auxiliary Account

A sub-account or category used in SAP account assignment to specify the type or purpose of purchase items, impacting tax treatment and calculation.

**Tags:** SAP, finance, accounting, purchasing

---

#### ME_FILL_KOMP_PO Function

An SAP function module that automatically determines the import indicator based on tax departure and destination countries during purchasing document processing.

**Tags:** SAP, function module, tax determination, purchasing

---

#### Goods Supplier Role

A SAP master data role designating the entity responsible for supplying goods, which determines the tax departure country for tax purposes.

The time period over which one-time investments in tax technology or processes are recovered through cost savings or value contributions.

---

#### Efficiencies (Tax Business Case)

Improvements in tax processes and operations that reduce cost and time while increasing effectiveness, contributing to the tax business case value.

---

#### Cash Potentials

Opportunities to improve cash flow through tax activities such as earlier VAT deductions and foreign VAT refunds, addressed within the tax business case.

Options to outsource or relocate tax activities, such as tax reporting, to distant or nearby geographic locations to optimize costs and operational efficiency.

Criteria in SAP that restrict access to certain condition records based on specific conditions, ensuring correct tax or price determination.

### Level 1 - Basic

#### Transfer Pricing Determination

Transfer pricing determination is the process of establishing arm's length prices for transactions between related parties based on accepted methodologies.

**Tags:** transfer pricing, tax compliance

**Prerequisites:** Transfer Pricing

---

#### Transfer Pricing Documentation

The collection of reports and filings, including CbCR, master files, and local files, that companies must prepare to demonstrate compliance with transfer pricing rules.

**Tags:** transfer pricing, tax compliance, documentation

**Prerequisites:** Transfer Pricing Management

**Related:** Transfer Pricing Testing

---

#### Transactional Profit Split Method

The transactional profit split method allocates combined profits from controlled transactions among associated enterprises based on their respective contributions.

**Tags:** transfer pricing, taxation, transfer pricing methods

**Prerequisites:** Transfer Pricing

---

#### Transactional Net Margin Method (TNMM)

A transfer pricing method comparing the net profit margin relative to an appropriate base (costs, sales, assets) from controlled transactions to those of comparable uncontrolled transactions.

**Also known as:** TNMM

**Tags:** transfer pricing, taxation, transfer pricing methods

**Prerequisites:** Transfer Pricing

---

#### Time-Dependent Tax

A time-dependent tax is a type of tax whose applicability or calculation depends on specific time factors, such as duration, timing, or periods.

**Tags:** taxation, time-based taxation

**Prerequisites:** Time Dependency

**Related:** Tax Determination

---

#### Third-Party Order

A third-party order is an order placed by or for a party that is neither the buyer nor the seller in a transaction, often involving intermediaries.

**Tags:** procurement, business processes

**Prerequisites:** Third-Party Process

**Related:** Tax Determination

---

#### Tax Amount

The calculated money value of tax payable based on the taxable base amount before or after discounts.

**Tags:** Accounting, Taxation, SAP, Finance

**Prerequisites:** Taxable Profit

**Related:** Tax Determination

---

#### Task List

A structured list of tasks created within SAP Tax Compliance to systematically remediate compliance hits, often based on pre-defined templates for efficiency.

**Also known as:** Compliance Task List

**Tags:** Operations Management, Maintenance, Task Management, Tax Compliance, Workflow

**Prerequisites:** Task Template

**Related:** Tax Monitoring Process

---

#### Stock Transport Order (STO)

A specialized purchase order document in SAP used to manage stock transfer processes between plants within the same or different company codes.

**Tags:** Inventory Management, Logistics, SAP

**Prerequisites:** Stock Transfer

**Related:** Storage Location, Item Categories

---

#### Standardization

The process of establishing and applying uniform procedures, definitions, and criteria to ensure consistency and comparability.

**Tags:** Business Process, Quality Management

**Prerequisites:** Standard Setup

---

#### Standard Ledger

A general ledger in SAP S/4HANA that serves as the primary ledger for recording financial transactions according to a specific accounting standard or principle.

**Also known as:** Fixed Ledger

**Tags:** Financial Accounting, Ledger Management, accounting, ledger, SAP S/4HANA

**Prerequisites:** Statement

**Related:** Special General Ledger Indicator, Standard Dimension

---

#### Spend Management

The process and tools used to control, optimize, and analyze procurement and spending within an organization.

**Tags:** Procurement, Financial Management

**Prerequisites:** Supplier Interaction

**Related:** Supplier Interaction, Tax Reporting Process

---

#### Condition types (in Sales and Distribution)

In SD, categories that specify different pricing elements such as discounts, surcharges, and taxes used during sales pricing.

**Tags:** SAP, Sales and Distribution, Pricing

**Prerequisites:** Sales and Distribution (SD) Overview

**Related:** Rounding rule, Routine

---

#### Record-to-report

An end-to-end finance process covering recording transactions through to generating financial statements and reports.

**Tags:** Finance, Accounting, Reporting

**Prerequisites:** Reconciliation account

**Related:** Tax Monitoring Process

---

#### Access sequences

SAP configurations defining the search strategy for condition records to determine applicable pricing or tax conditions.

**Tags:** Pricing, Sales and Distribution, SAP, tax

**Prerequisites:** Condition tables

---

#### Purchase order

An external SAP document representing a formal request to procure goods or services, containing details on vendor, pricing, purchasing organization, and tax-relevant information used for tax determination.

**Also known as:** PO

**Tags:** Procurement, Purchasing, business process, SAP, document, tax, Materials Management, SAP S/4HANA

**Prerequisites:** Purchase requisition

**Related:** Tax Determination

---

#### Procure-to-pay

An end-to-end business process covering all activities from requisitioning and purchasing goods or services to payment processing.

**Also known as:** P2P

**Tags:** Procurement, Finance, Supply Chain, SAP, financial processes

**Prerequisites:** Procurement

**Related:** Proof of delivery, Provider of information

---

#### Intercompany billing

Intercompany billing is the process of invoicing between two company codes within the same corporate group for goods or services transferred between them.

**Also known as:** Intercompany invoicing, IC Billing

**Tags:** Procurement, Accounting, SAP, Billing, Intercompany Transactions, finance, corporate management

**Prerequisites:** Number range

**Related:** Transaction Matrix

Material master is a central repository of all information related to materials that supports procurement, inventory management, and valuation.

**Prerequisites:** Material

**Related:** Material tax classification, Material group

---

#### Material Ledger

A ledger in SAP S/4HANA that stores detailed information related to materials management, including all material movements captured in a single table (MATDOC).

**Prerequisites:** Material movement

An analytical process to verify the completeness and correctness of tax-relevant master data in SAP S/4HANA to support accurate tax determination and compliance.

**Prerequisites:** Master data

**Related:** Tax Monitoring Process

---

Set up of a ledger involves configuring the ledger parameters and settings to enable proper recording and management of financial data.

**Also known as:** Ledger configuration

**Tags:** accounting, ledger, configuration

**Prerequisites:** Ledger

---

#### Tax Code

A tax code defines the tax treatment of a business transaction, typically including attributes such as tax rate, tax account, and a description to determine applicable taxes.

**Also known as:** Tax Classification Code, Tax Indicator, Tax Codes, Indirect Tax Code

**Related:** Inactive Tax Code, Target Tax Code, Advance return

A central master data object in SAP representing a vendor, customer, or other business entity; in India GST context, plants can be set up as business partners with vendor and customer roles and tax numbers.

**Tags:** SAP, Master Data, Business Partner, GST

**Prerequisites:** Master data management

**Related:** Multiple GST IDs for Business Partners

---

#### Condition Technique

A method in SAP pricing and tax determination that uses condition tables, access sequences, and condition records to determine prices, discounts, surcharges, or taxes dynamically in sales documents.

**Also known as:** Condition-Based Determination, Condition Logic

**Prerequisites:** Default place of delivery

**Related:** Transport responsibility, Tax Determination

---

#### Intrastat report

Intrastat report is the standardized monthly statistical declaration submitted by EU businesses to record the movement of goods between member states for trade monitoring and compliance.
