**Tags:** Intrastat, Trade compliance, Statistics, Tax reporting, EU trade, Compliance, Statistical report, European Union, trade statistics, Reporting, Customs

---

Define Procedure is the SAP Intrastat configuration step to set or check statistical procedures related to trade reporting, largely deprecated since 2022 changes.

**Also known as:** Procedure definition

**Tags:** SAP, Intrastat, Configuration

**Prerequisites:** Statistical procedure (Intrastat)

**Related:** Tax Monitoring Process

---

#### Real-Time Tax Reporting

The process of submitting tax-related transactional data like invoices or sales information continuously or in near real-time to tax authorities to increase transparency and enable faster tax administration.

**Also known as:** Near Real-Time Tax Reporting, Continuous Transaction Controls (CTC)

**Tags:** tax compliance, digital reporting, Tax Reporting, Compliance, Digital Tax

**Prerequisites:** Mandatory E-Invoicing

**Related:** Tax Authorities

---

An integrated SAP solution within SAP S/4HANA that manages global tax functions including e-invoicing, real-time and statutory reporting, combining document compliance and advanced tax reporting capabilities.

---

#### Tax Reporting

The process of compiling and submitting tax information to authorities, covering both direct and indirect taxes, using various solutions and compliance frameworks.

**Prerequisites:** Taxpayers

The state of preparedness to undergo tax or financial audits, ensuring accurate historic data retention and an audit trail for examination by authorities.

**Also known as:** Tax Audit Preparedness

**Tags:** tax compliance, audit

**Prerequisites:** Tax Audit Period

**Related:** Tax Monitoring Process

---

#### Tax Compliance

The adherence to relevant tax laws and regulations facilitated by SAP S/4HANA solutions through automated reporting, calculation, and risk management features.

**Also known as:** Tax Adherence, Regulatory Tax Compliance, Tax Regulatory Compliance

**Tags:** taxation, legal compliance, tax, regulation, business process, regulatory compliance, Tax Management, SAP, Compliance, legal

**Prerequisites:** Taxpayers

**Related:** Tax Compliance Processes

---

#### Transfer Pricing Master Data

Data related to transfer pricing rules, settings, or segments in SAP systems, which serve as a basis for transfer pricing reporting and simulation but may have reporting limitations natively in SAP S/4HANA.

**Tags:** SAP S/4HANA, Master Data, Transfer Pricing

**Prerequisites:** Operational Transfer Pricing System

**Related:** Tax Data Model

---

#### Custom Fields

User-defined additional fields that can be created and added to standard SAP tables such as ACDOCA to extend data structures and enhance reporting and business processes.

**Also known as:** User-Defined Fields

**Tags:** SAP S/4HANA, Extensibility, Data Model

**Prerequisites:** Transaction SCFD_EUI

**Related:** Custom Fields and Logic app

---

#### Single Version of Truth

Single version of truth is a unified and consistent data source created by aggregating and harmonizing data across systems for reliable reporting and decision-making.

**Tags:** Data Management, Reporting

**Prerequisites:** SAP BW/4HANA

---

#### Transactional Parameters

Transactional parameters are variables such as profit markup or transaction volumes that can be adjusted in simulations to analyze their impact on transfer pricing outcomes.

**Tags:** Transfer Pricing, Simulation Variables

**Prerequisites:** Simulation (What-if Scenarios)

**Related:** Tax Determination

---

#### ABAP CDS

ABAP Core Data Services is a technology used to create virtual data models that represent operational data within SAP systems.

**Also known as:** Core Data Services (CDS)

**Tags:** SAP Technology, Data Modeling

**Prerequisites:** Virtual Data Model

**Related:** Tax Data Model

---

#### Segmented Profit and Loss (Segmented P&L)

A financial report that breaks down profitability by different dimensions, essential for monitoring and analyzing transfer prices and margins within a corporate group.

**Also known as:** Segmented Income Statement

**Tags:** accounting, financial reporting, transfer pricing

**Prerequisites:** Universal Journal

---

#### Transfer Price Monitoring

Ongoing review and analysis of transfer prices applied to intercompany transactions to ensure compliance with a group’s transfer pricing policy and to identify deviations requiring correction.

**Tags:** transfer pricing, compliance, financial monitoring

**Prerequisites:** Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) for Transfer Pricing

---

#### Resale-Minus Method

A transfer pricing approach that determines the transfer price by deducting an appropriate gross margin from the resale price to an independent party.

**Also known as:** Resale Price Method, Resale price minus method

**Tags:** transfer pricing, pricing methods

**Prerequisites:** Purchase price

**Related:** Transfer Price Determination

---

#### Standard cost

Predetermined average cost of a material, used for valuation and costing, fixed until updated by a new cost estimate.

**Tags:** costing, valuation methods, SAP

**Prerequisites:** Cost estimate

**Related:** Moving average price, Taxable Profit

---

#### Routings

Descriptions of the sequence of operations or manufacturing steps to produce a product, including associated costs.

**Tags:** manufacturing, SAP, costing

**Prerequisites:** Transaction CA03

**Related:** Bill of materials (BOM)

---

#### Bill of materials (BOM)

A comprehensive list of raw materials, components, and instructions required to manufacture a product, which can be utilized in tax solution planning and implementations within SAP.

**Also known as:** BOM

**Tags:** production, SAP, costing, materials management, Manufacturing, Tax Planning, Supply Chain

**Prerequisites:** Transaction CS03

**Related:** Routings, Tax prices

---

#### Adjusted market price

The market price modified by specific adjustments to reflect comparability for transfer pricing purposes.

**Tags:** transfer pricing, valuation

**Prerequisites:** Transfer pricing function

**Related:** External benchmarking

---

#### Tax regulations

Legal rules governing how costs and prices are treated for taxation purposes.

**Also known as:** Tax laws, Tax rules

**Tags:** taxation, regulation

**Prerequisites:** Tax prices

---

#### Total cost

The complete aggregation of all production-related costs, including material, conversion, and general administrative costs.

**Tags:** cost accounting, costing

**Prerequisites:** Full Cost Approach

**Related:** Total conversion cost, Total material cost, General and administration and other cost

---

#### Sales Price

The price at which goods or services are sold in intercompany transactions, used in transfer pricing calculations.

**Tags:** transfer pricing, pricing, sales transactions

**Prerequisites:** Gross margin

**Related:** Transfer pricing function

---

#### Central Coordination in Transfer Pricing

The management approach ensuring unified control and harmonization across all subprocesses of operational transfer pricing for consistency, efficiency, and compliance.

**Tags:** governance, transfer pricing, process management

**Prerequisites:** Single Source of Truth

**Related:** Tax Reporting Process

---

#### Static Transfer Pricing Models

Transfer pricing models lacking flexibility and adaptability to changing business dynamics or tax regulations due to limitations in built-in solutions or spreadsheet complexity.

**Tags:** transfer pricing, modeling, system limitations

**Prerequisites:** Spreadsheet-Based Processes

---

#### Simulation of Reports

Analytical processes performing "what-if" and "how-to" scenarios on transfer price adjustments or volume changes to predict financial impacts.

**Tags:** transfer pricing, simulation, decision support

**Prerequisites:** Central Data Repository

**Related:** Tax Reporting Process, Scenarios (in planning), Planning, Parallel Evaluations

---

#### Transfer Pricing Adjustments and True-Up Report

A report that identifies adjustments to transfer prices ensuring tax compliance by aligning revenue and expense corrections within the reporting period, showing variance between targeted and actual profitability.

**Also known as:** True-Up Report, Transfer Pricing Adjustments Report

**Tags:** transfer pricing, compliance, financial reporting

**Prerequisites:** Complex International Tax Regulations

**Related:** Prospective Price Adjustments, Tax Reporting Process

---

#### Penalties

Financial or administrative sanctions imposed by tax authorities for violations of transfer pricing regulations and noncompliance.

**Tags:** tax law, transfer pricing compliance

**Prerequisites:** Noncompliance

---

#### Intercompany Sales Orders

Sales orders created within an enterprise to document and process sales transactions between related entities, e.g., in SAP via transaction VA01.

**Tags:** ERP systems, sales management, transfer pricing

**Prerequisites:** Financial Accounting Invoices

**Related:** Intracompany Transactions

#### Net Profits from Intercompany Transactions

Financial outcome representing the net income generated from transfers within a multinational group, critical for transfer pricing and tax evaluations.

**Tags:** finance, transfer pricing

---

#### Legal Regulation

Statutory and regulatory rules that govern how transfer prices must be set and reported to comply with jurisdictional requirements.

**Tags:** law, compliance, transfer pricing

---

#### Intracompany Transactions

Transfers of goods, services, or funds between different units or divisions within the same legal entity.

---

#### Arm’s Length Principle

A standard in transfer pricing requiring that prices charged between related parties be consistent with those charged between independent parties under similar circumstances.

**Tags:** transfer pricing, tax law, taxation

**Prerequisites:** OECD Framework

---

#### Coordination

The alignment and harmonization of activities and decisions within an organization to meet management objectives, including setting incentives and facilitating decision making.

**Tags:** management, business processes

**Prerequisites:** Management

---

#### Legal Transfer Prices

Prices set for transactions between related parties that comply with tax regulations and legal requirements, ensuring arm’s length principles are met.

#### Define Region

The SAP configuration step to specify subnational geographical regions within a country that are relevant for Intrastat reporting, such as German regions.

**Also known as:** Region definition

**Tags:** SAP configuration, Intrastat, Geography

**Prerequisites:** Define Conversion of Region

**Related:** Tax Determination

---

#### Country of Origin

The country in which goods are produced or manufactured, considered one of the options for determining tax liability in cross-border transactions.

**Also known as:** Origin country

**Tags:** Intrastat configuration, Tax reporting, international trade, taxation, indirect tax

**Prerequisites:** Define Exceptions for Mapping of Country of Origin

**Related:** Tax Determination

---

#### Define Exceptions for Conversion of Partner Country

Configuration step in SAP to set up mappings for special regions or jurisdictions to Intrastat countries, such as linking Northern Ireland to a specific country code.

**Also known as:** Partner Country mapping exceptions

**Tags:** SAP configuration, Intrastat

**Prerequisites:** Northern Ireland treaty

---

#### European Commission (EC) Sales List

A tax report required in the EU listing intra-community supplies of goods and services for VAT purposes.

**Also known as:** EC Sales List

**Tags:** Tax reporting, EU regulations, VAT

**Prerequisites:** Value-added tax (VAT) ID

**Related:** European Union (EU), Tax Reporting Process

---

#### Tax Code Allocation

The assignment of appropriate tax codes to transactional data, such as invoices and sales documents, to ensure correct tax calculation and reporting.

**Tags:** Taxation, Accounting Process, Tax Code

**Prerequisites:** Automated Accounts Payable VAT Determination

---

#### Correction Steps

Actions taken to address and remediate tax compliance issues or exceptions identified during monitoring and controls.

**Also known as:** Mitigation Steps

**Tags:** tax compliance, risk mitigation

**Prerequisites:** Three-Way Match

---

#### Tax Control Catalog

A documented collection of specific controls designed to mitigate identified tax risks and ensure compliance within an organization.

**Also known as:** Control Catalog

**Tags:** tax compliance, internal controls

**Prerequisites:** Compliance Requirements

**Related:** Tax Control System

---

#### Tax Decisions/Determination

The process of applying tax rules to transaction data to establish tax liabilities or entitlements, such as taxes due or deductible input VAT.

**Also known as:** Tax Calculation, Tax Determination

**Tags:** tax compliance, tax technology

**Prerequisites:** VAT Treatment

**Related:** Withholding Taxes (WHT)

#### Tax Technology

The use of digital tools, software, and methodologies to manage, automate, and optimize tax-related functions and compliance within organizations.

**Also known as:** Tax IT Solutions, Tax Tech, TaxTech

**Tags:** tax technology, ERP, tax compliance, tax, technology, digital transformation, compliance, automation

**Prerequisites:** Digitization of Tax Functions

**Related:** Data-Driven Tax Function

---

#### Tax Burden

The total amount of tax liability borne by an entity, often determined based on tax rates applied to the tax base.

**Tags:** tax, finance

**Prerequisites:** Tax Base

**Related:** Tax Positions, Fiscal Cash Flow

---

#### Tax Audits

The process of reviewing and evaluating a company's tax records and compliance by tax authorities, including assessment of findings and their financial impact.

**Tags:** tax compliance, audit, Taxation, Compliance

**Prerequisites:** Transparency of Tax Data

---

#### Tax Provisions

Accrued amounts in accounting representing probable tax obligations; distinguished from tax liabilities that are due amounts, often requiring specific presentation under international accounting standards.

**Tags:** tax, finance, accounting, International Accounting Standards

**Prerequisites:** Tax Data

**Related:** Tax Loss Carryback, Tax Loss Carryforward

---

#### Tax Receivables

Amounts of tax expected to be collected from tax authorities or other parties, tracked and monitored as part of tax financial management.

**Tags:** tax, finance, accounting

**Prerequisites:** Tax Data

---

The continuous processing and analysis of large volumes of data, often automated through predefined routines, to support operational tax controls and audits.

**Also known as:** Mass Analysis

**Tags:** data analysis, automation, tax control

**Prerequisites:** Heterogeneous System Landscape

**Related:** E-audits

A technique that uses actual data to create forecasts or budget plans to guide future financial or operational decisions.

**Also known as:** Budget Planning, Forecasting

**Tags:** planning, analytics, finance, business planning, financial forecasting

**Prerequisites:** Predictive Analytics

---

#### Tax Return

A formal report submitted to tax authorities detailing taxable transactions, tax collected (output tax), tax paid on inputs (input tax), and other relevant tax information during a specific period.

**Also known as:** Tax Declaration

**Tags:** tax, filing, compliance, taxation, reporting

**Prerequisites:** Tax-relevant Transactions

---

#### Account Movements

Changes in account balances over a period, analyzed to detect tax-relevant transactions or anomalies.

**Also known as:** Account Activity

**Related:** Workflow Concept in SAP Tax Compliance, My Compliance Tasks App, Tax Controls and Corrective Actions

**Prerequisites:** General Ledger Account Master Data

Condition types are individual pricing elements within a pricing procedure, such as discounts or tax types like MWST (output tax), each with specific properties affecting pricing determination.

#### eDocument Instance

An electronic representation of a source document stored in the SAP system database that contains all necessary data to generate, submit, and monitor tax reporting documents electronically.

**Also known as:** Electronic Document Instance

**Tags:** Electronic Document Processing, SAP System, Tax Compliance

**Prerequisites:** Source Document

**Related:** Electronic Document (eDocument) Processing

---

#### Electronic Document (eDocument) Processing

The creation, submission, mapping, and monitoring of legally compliant business documents in electronic format, enabling automation and compliance with Continuous Transaction Controls.

**Also known as:** eDocument Processing Framework

**Tags:** Tax Compliance, Automation, Document Management, SAP S/4HANA

**Prerequisites:** XML Format in Tax Reporting

---

#### Post payable activity (Tax Payable Posting)

The post payable activity is the process of paying tax amounts by posting balances from input and output tax accounts to a VAT payable account, requiring proper general ledger configuration and user authorization.

**Also known as:** Tax payable posting

**Tags:** SAP, Financial Accounting, Tax Payment

**Prerequisites:** General Ledger Data on the Line Item Level

---

#### Tax code mapping

Tax code mapping is the process of assigning tax codes along with account keys to specific group numbers that correspond either to tax base fields or tax amount fields on VAT returns to ensure accurate tax reporting.

**Also known as:** Tax code to group number assignment

**Tags:** SAP, Financial Accounting, Tax Configuration

**Prerequisites:** Tax groups

**Related:** Tax box

---

#### Report category

A report category groups different versions of a report used by the system to generate concrete reports for specific periods and organizational units, often reflecting changes in legislation that lead to new versions.

**Also known as:** Reporting category

**Tags:** SAP, Reporting, Tax Compliance

**Prerequisites:** Reporting entity

**Related:** Tax group version

---

#### Paradigm Shift 2

The shift from Level 4 (E-Audit) to Level 5 (E-Assess) where tax authorities perform real-time electronic tax assessments without requiring taxpayers to file declarations.

**Also known as:** Second Paradigm Shift

**Tags:** Tax Technology, E-Assess, Digital Transformation

**Prerequisites:** Level 4: E-Audit

---

#### Level 2: E-Accounting

A maturity level where taxpayers submit accounting or source data electronically on a defined timetable to support filings, with frequent changes to data formats and submissions.

**Also known as:** E-Accounting

**Tags:** Tax Reporting, Accounting, Digital Tax Compliance

**Prerequisites:** Level 1: E-File

---

#### Solution: Customer Tax Classification by Vendor Tax ID

A method where the tax classification field (TAXK1) is set based on presence or absence of a vendor tax ID (STCEG). If the tax ID exists, TAXK1 is set to 1 (taxable); otherwise, it is set to 'N' (natural person).

**Tags:** SAP, Materials Management, Taxation, Customization

**Prerequisites:** Vendor Tax ID (STCEG)

**Related:** Tax Classification (TAXK1)

---

A user exit in SAP (Program MV45AFZB) with sub-exits like USEREXIT_NEW_PRICING_VBAP and USEREXIT_NEW_PRICING_VBKD that enable triggering of new pricing calculations during sales order processing.

**Tags:** SAP, User Exit, Pricing, Sales Order

**Prerequisites:** Sales Order (SAP)

**Related:** KNVL Table

---

#### Tax Exemption License

A license recorded in SAP that enables tax exemption functionality, often represented by a condition type (e.g., LCIT) and maintained at the condition level to indicate eligibility for tax exemptions.

**Also known as:** Tax Exemption Certificate, Exemption License

**Tags:** SAP, Tax Exemption, Licenses, Condition Types

**Prerequisites:** Customer Business Partner Role

**Related:** KNVL Table

---

#### Nexus (Tax)

A taxpayer's sufficient physical or economic presence in a state that creates a tax obligation for collecting and remitting sales and use tax.

**Also known as:** Tax Nexus

**Tags:** taxation, United States, sales tax

**Prerequisites:** Sales and Use Tax

**Related:** Flash Title Theory

---

#### 0% Indirect Tax

An exemption or zero rate applied to indirect taxes like VAT or GST on certain eligible transactions, such as exports or intra-community supplies.

**Also known as:** Zero percent VAT, Zero percent GST

**Tags:** taxation, indirect tax, exemption

**Prerequisites:** Export Responsibility

**Related:** Cross-Border Chain Transaction

A standard SAP condition table (Taxes: Plant, Account Assignment, Origin and Region) used for tax determination when detailed account assignment exists.

**Prerequisites:** Account Assignment

---

**Prerequisites:** Vendor Master Data

Classification codes in SAP EDI mapping where LI refers to vendor partners and LS refers to logical systems like pre-systems or external systems.

**Prerequisites:** Pricing Communication Structures

Processing routines specify the program and smart form used to generate print output for a given output type, with a FORM routine typically called ENTRY for execution.

**Prerequisites:** Transaction NACE

**Related:** Smart Forms

The process by which the tax ID number shown in the billing document in Sales and Distribution is copied to the corresponding accounting document in Financial Accounting at the item level.

The date usually representing the supply of goods or services; used for delivery-related or service billing in SAP.

---

Standard reports related to indirect tax in SAP typically use the posting date rather than the document date to determine reporting periods.

A setting in SAP sales documents that defines the processing of individual schedule lines related to delivery and billing.

---

#### Sales Area

An organizational unit in SAP representing a combination of sales organization, distribution channel, and division, used to organize sales processes and determine conditions and taxes.

A consumption tax levied on sales and services, handled in SAP by specific tax procedures and classification methods.

Settings and rules used in SAP to identify the correct tax codes and calculations applicable to transactions based on factors like country, tax category, and product classification.

#### Exchange Rate Determination According to Tax Reporting Date

An SAP configuration option that allows the exchange rate to be determined based on the tax reporting date instead of posting or document date, synchronized with tax reporting requirements.

**Tags:** Financial Accounting, SAP, Exchange Rates, Tax Reporting

**Prerequisites:** Exchange Rates

**Related:** Tax Reporting Process

---

#### Tax Reporting Date Activation

The process in SAP Financial Accounting of enabling the tax reporting date for a company code, making VATDATE mandatory for tax-relevant documents and allowing specific tax date determination.

**Tags:** Financial Accounting, SAP Configuration, Tax Reporting

**Prerequisites:** Tax Reporting Date (VATDATE)

---

#### Substantive Audit Procedures

Detailed testing in tax audits designed to verify the accuracy and completeness of transactions and account balances when compliance testing is insufficient.

**Tags:** Audit, Tax Compliance

**Prerequisites:** Audit Evidence

**Related:** Compliance Testing

---

#### Income Inclusion Rule (IIR)

A GloBE rule requiring parent corporations to include in their taxable income the low-taxed profits of controlled foreign subsidiaries, applying a top-up tax to meet a minimum tax rate.

**Also known as:** IIR

**Tags:** International Taxation, Anti-Base Erosion

**Prerequisites:** Controlled Foreign Corporation (CFC) Rules

**Related:** Top-Up Tax

---

#### Tax Competition

The practice by jurisdictions to attract businesses by offering lower tax rates or favorable tax treatments, often leading to reduced global tax revenues.

**Tags:** Tax Policy, International Taxation

**Prerequisites:** Jurisdictional Sovereignty

**Related:** Profit Shifting

---

#### Global Effective Minimum Tax

A tax rate floor applied globally to ensure multinational corporations pay a minimum level of tax on profits, reducing profit shifting and harmful competition.

**Also known as:** Minimum Taxation, Global Minimum Tax

**Tags:** International Taxation, Tax Policy

**Prerequisites:** Top-Up Tax

---

#### Consolidated Financial Accounts

Financial statements that combine the financials of a parent company and its subsidiaries into a single report to reflect the financial position of the entire group.

**Tags:** Accounting, Corporate Finance

**Prerequisites:** Multinational Enterprise (MNE) Group

**Related:** Aggregation and Consolidation

---

A widely used framework developed by the OECD that provides guidance for the application of international tax treaties between countries.

**Also known as:** OECD Model Tax Convention

**Tags:** International Tax Law, Tax Treaties

**Prerequisites:** Permanent Establishment (PE)

---

#### Market Jurisdiction

A country or region where a multinational enterprise's goods or services are consumed or sold, granting it a basis to tax profits under Pillar One.

**Tags:** Taxation, International Tax Law

**Prerequisites:** Amount A

---

#### BEPS 2.0

A global initiative to address Base Erosion and Profit Shifting challenges by multinational companies through a two-pillar reform of the international tax system.

**Also known as:** BEPS Project 2.0

**Tags:** International Taxation, Tax Policy

**Prerequisites:** World Tax Order

Differences between the tax base of an asset or liability and its carrying amount in the financial statements that will reverse in future periods.

Regulations such as US-GAAP and IFRS that mandate how financial data should be reported, aiming to provide useful information for investors and stakeholders.

#### Base Erosion and Profit Shifting (BEPS)

A set of tax avoidance strategies used by multinational companies to shift profits from high-tax to low-tax jurisdictions, eroding the tax base of the higher-tax countries.

**Tags:** international tax, tax avoidance, OECD, tax policy, tax, direct tax, taxation

**Prerequisites:** OECD

#### Standard Reporting Requirements

Mandatory periodic filings such as tax returns required by tax authorities to document and remit indirect taxes like VAT or sales tax, varying by country and influenced by factors such as the tax gap.

**Tags:** taxation, compliance, reporting, indirect tax

#### VAT Liability

The legal obligation of a party in a transaction to pay or remit value-added tax to the tax authorities, which depends on the nature of the transaction and local tax regulations.

**Related:** Drop Shipping, Tax Reporting Date (BKPF-VATDATE)

The location where a taxable event such as work delivery, supply, or work performance is deemed to occur for tax purposes, affecting VAT liability and exemptions.

**Tags:** taxation, VAT, transaction location

**Prerequisites:** EU VAT Directive

---

#### Work Delivery

In the EU context, the supply involving processing or working on goods that do not belong to the supplier, distinguished from normal supply and work supply.

**Also known as:** Work Supply

**Tags:** taxation, European Union, VAT, work performance, Project Management, Operations

**Prerequisites:** Work Performance

---

#### Tax Exemptions

Transactions or events that fall under indirect tax law but are exempt from tax under specific legal provisions, thus not subject to tax collection though still regulated.

**Prerequisites:** Taxable Event

A system of organizations, people, activities, information, and resources involved in supplying a product or service to a consumer.

**Related:** Procure-to-Pay Scenario

---

#### Reverse charge mechanism

A VAT procedure in intra-EU B2B transactions where the recipient of goods or services is liable to pay VAT instead of the supplier, simplifying cross-border tax collection.

**Also known as:** Reverse VAT charge

**Tags:** VAT, international tax, cross-border transactions

**Prerequisites:** Intra-community supply (EU VAT)

---

#### Neutrality in competition (VAT context)

The principle ensuring that VAT systems do not distort competition by imposing equal tax burdens on similar goods and services irrespective of production or distribution chain length.

**Also known as:** Tax neutrality in VAT

**Tags:** tax policy, VAT, competition law

**Prerequisites:** VAT Directive 2006/112/EC

**Related:** Value-added tax (VAT)

---

#### Value-added tax (VAT)

An indirect tax imposed at each stage of production or distribution, calculated on the taxable amount usually equal to the full amount paid for goods or services, with the ability for suppliers to deduct input VAT.

**Also known as:** VAT

**Prerequisites:** Taxable amount

#### One Single Data Source

A unified repository providing consolidated tax-relevant data accessible for reporting and analysis, enhancing efficiency and self-service capabilities.

**Also known as:** Single Source of Truth

**Tags:** data management, tax compliance, automation

**Prerequisites:** ERP/Legacy System Source Data Integrity

**Related:** Transparency (Tax Transparency)

#### IT Enablement (Tax Function)

Incorporating information technology tools and systems in tax functions to enhance efficiency, automation, and decision-making.

**Tags:** taxation, technology, automation

**Prerequisites:** Automation (in Tax Reporting)

---

#### Strategic Business Partner (Tax Function)

A modern role of tax departments acting as integral contributors to business strategy and decision-making beyond compliance and reporting.

**Also known as:** Value-Driven Tax Function

**Tags:** taxation, business strategy, organizational roles

**Prerequisites:** Decision Support (Tax)

---

#### tax determination rules

Rules configured in SAP to define how VAT IDs are selected and applied for different business partner roles and countries in tax calculation scenarios.

**Tags:** SAP configuration, tax compliance

**Prerequisites:** Sales Organization Level Tax ID Determination Configuration

**Related:** Goods Supplier Role, User Exits for VAT ID Determination

---

#### Import Indicators in SAP

Classification values indicating the nature of a purchasing transaction: 0 = domestic, 1 = cross-border non-EU import, 2 = intra-community acquisition within EU.

**Also known as:** Import Indicator Values

**Tags:** SAP, indirect tax, purchasing, import

**Prerequisites:** ME_FILL_KOMP_PO Function

**Related:** Tax Indicator Import

---

#### Construction Services

Services related to construction projects, which in the EU are typically subject to the reverse charge mechanism for indirect tax purposes.

**Tags:** taxation, EU law, indirect tax, construction

**Prerequisites:** Reverse Charge Regulation

**Related:** Indirect tax return

---

#### Rule D for VAT ID Determination

A SAP VAT ID determination rule that generally takes the VAT ID and tax classification from the ship-to party according to the tax country of destination.

**Also known as:** Ship-to Party Rule

**Tags:** SAP, VAT ID Determination, tax configuration

**Prerequisites:** Business Partner Roles for Tax ID Determination

**Related:** VAT ID Determination Rules (SAP)

---

#### Rule C for VAT ID Determination

A SAP VAT ID determination variant of the default rule where, if sold-to and ship-to differ and sold-to has a VAT ID for the tax country, that VAT ID is chosen.

**Also known as:** Enhanced Default Rule

**Tags:** SAP, VAT ID Determination, tax configuration

**Prerequisites:** Business Partner Roles for Tax ID Determination

**Related:** VAT ID Determination Rules (SAP)

---

#### Rule B for VAT ID Determination

A SAP VAT ID determination rule that generally takes the VAT ID and tax classification from the payer according to the tax country of destination.

**Also known as:** Payer Rule

**Tags:** SAP, VAT ID Determination, tax configuration

**Prerequisites:** Business Partner Roles for Tax ID Determination

**Related:** VAT ID Determination Rules (SAP)

---

#### Rule A for VAT ID Determination

A SAP VAT ID determination rule that generally takes the VAT ID and tax classification from the sold-to party according to the tax country of destination.

**Also known as:** Sold-to Party Rule

**Tags:** SAP, VAT ID Determination, tax configuration

**Prerequisites:** Business Partner Roles for Tax ID Determination

**Related:** VAT ID Determination Rules (SAP)

---

#### Tax Number Category Function Name

A validation function assigned to a tax number category in SAP that defines the proofing logic such as length, format, and checksum applied to tax IDs.

**Also known as:** Validation Function

**Tags:** SAP, tax compliance, validation

**Prerequisites:** Tax ID Validation

---

#### Tax Identification Number (Tax ID)

A unique identifier assigned to natural or legal persons for tax purposes, necessary for tax reporting and compliance in many jurisdictions.

**Also known as:** Tax Number, TIN, VAT ID (where applicable)

**Tags:** taxation, identification, SAP, compliance

**Prerequisites:** Tax ID Maintenance in SAP

---

Additional field in SAP tax codes indicating the reporting country, essential for tax reporting by jurisdiction after plant abroad activation.

**Tags:** SAP, Tax code, Reporting, Country

**Prerequisites:** Tax Reporting Country functionality

**Related:** Tax Data Model

SAP transaction code used to define or modify item categories within sales documents, including those for stock transfers.

**Tags:** SAP, Transaction Code, Sales and Distribution, Configuration

**Prerequisites:** Item category NLN

---

Condition representing input tax in the country of destination for intra-community acquisition of goods used in SAP internal stock transfer postings.

Encapsulated programming constructs in ABAP representing entities with attributes and methods, used to model components within SAP customizations.

An SAP transaction code used in purchasing to create condition records for pricing.

**Prerequisites:** Field Catalog

**Related:** Tax Code Reporting Country

**Prerequisites:** WHT Type

**Related:** Tax Code Reporting Country

#### Statutory Reporting

A legal requirement for preparing tax-related and financial reports that include determination of current and deferred taxes along with disclosures such as tax risks.

**Prerequisites:** Subject to Tax Rule, Legal Entities

#### Mapping between SAP regions and Intrastat regions

Mapping between SAP regions and Intrastat regions is the process of aligning SAP territorial codes with the official Intrastat regions to ensure consistent trade reporting.

**Also known as:** Region mapping, SAP-Intrastat region mapping

**Tags:** SAP, Intrastat, Data mapping, Trade compliance

**Prerequisites:** SAP regions, Intrastat regions

**Related:** Tax Reporting Process

#### Indirect Allocation

A type of cost allocation where all key figure amounts from sender to receiver are distributed leveraging a cost driver on the receiver side without requiring matching characteristics on sender and receiver.

**Tags:** Cost Allocation, SAP Profitability and Performance Management, Allocation Type

**Prerequisites:** Sender and Receiver (Allocation Concepts), Allocation Function

---

#### Direct Allocation

A type of cost allocation where key figures are distributed from sender to receiver based on matching characteristics on both sides and a cost driver defined on the receiver side.

**Tags:** Cost Allocation, SAP Profitability and Performance Management, Allocation Type, accounting, tax allocation

**Prerequisites:** Sender and Receiver (Allocation Concepts), Allocation Function

---

#### Pricing Procedures

A set of rules in SAP defining how prices, discounts, surcharges, and taxes are determined and calculated in sales and purchasing processes.

**Also known as:** Price Determination Procedure, Pricing Rules, Price Determination Procedures

**Tags:** SAP, pricing, sales and distribution, sales

**Prerequisites:** Access Sequence, Transaction V/08

**Related:** Pricing conditions, Customer/country discounts, Material-specific markups, Country-specific markups, Sales and distribution

---

An SAP costing transaction (CK40N) used to calculate planned cost estimates for materials based on cost components and structures.

**Tags:** SAP, costing, transactions

**Prerequisites:** Costing variants, Transaction CK11N

**Related:** Transaction CK40N

---

A defined set of activities to calculate current and deferred taxes, mapping accounts, and reconciling tax expenses within tax reporting solutions like SAP Profitability and Performance Management.

Accounting entries recorded to reflect tax obligations, payments, or adjustments in the financial system.

#### Rule-Based Statutory-to-Tax Adjustments

Adjustments made to reconcile differences between statutory financial accounting and tax accounting by applying rules to posted local and tax GAAP values in SAP S/4HANA.

**Also known as:** Statutory-to-Tax Adjustments

**Tags:** tax accounting, SAP S/4HANA, financial adjustments, GAAP reconciliation

**Prerequisites:** Tax GAAP, Local GAAP

**Related:** Tax Rules, Tax Ledger Hub app, Approval and Posting Process, Account Split Feature

The condition amount is the monetary value determined by a condition record applied during pricing or tax calculations in SAP.

**Prerequisites:** Condition record, Condition base value

---

#### Tax Calculation Methods

Prescribed formulas or approaches used to compute the amount of withholding tax based on the tax base, taxable portion, and applicable rates.

**Tags:** Taxation, Withholding Tax

**Prerequisites:** Taxable Portion, WHT Rate

**Related:** Basic WHT Function

---

Financial reports that aggregate the financial information of a parent company and its subsidiaries prepared according to international or other accounting standards.

**Tags:** accounting, financial reporting

#### Tax Connector

An ABAP-based add-on installed on-premise that acts as an interface managing data exchange between SAP S/4HANA and an external tax engine, including data collection and writing results back to SAP documents.

**Also known as:** Tax Connector Add-On

**Tags:** SAP Add-On, Integration Layer, Indirect Tax

**Prerequisites:** External Tax Engine, Data Layer in Tax Engine Architecture

---

#### SAP Tax Service

An SAP-provided cloud-based external tax calculation engine (formerly SAP Localization Hub) for determining indirect taxes considering country- and region-specific tax rules, accessible via SAP Integration Suite.

**Also known as:** SAP Localization Hub, SAP External Tax Service

**Tags:** SAP, Indirect Tax, Cloud Service, Tax Calculation Engine, tax engine, technology

**Prerequisites:** Enterprise Global Account, SAP Integration Suite

**Related:** Tax Service Extension via IF_TXS_CLASS_ENHANCEMENT

---

#### Taxable Transactions

Taxable transactions are business transactions that legally require the application of indirect tax based on relevant tax laws and regulations.

**Tags:** taxation

**Prerequisites:** Tax Classifications, Tax Point

---

#### Reconciliation Account for Customer Down Payments

A general ledger account specifically assigned to handle down payments received from customers, requiring specific tax category settings for correct output tax processing.

**Also known as:** Down Payment GL Account

**Tags:** Financial Accounting, SAP General Ledger, Tax Management

**Prerequisites:** Transaction FS00, Tax Category +B (Output Tax – down payments managed gross)

#### International Tax Order

Regulations and directives governing the application and collection of taxes across international borders.

**Tags:** taxation, international law

**Prerequisites:** Jurisdiction, Income Inclusion Rule

**Related:** International Trade, Intercompany Profit, Insourcing

---

#### Transactional Data

Data recorded during business transactions, such as sales orders or invoices, which is derived from or supplemented by master data for tax processing.

**Also known as:** Transaction Data, Process data

**Tags:** tax data, business transactions, Financial Data

**Prerequisites:** Master Data (Tax), Operational Processes (Order-to-Cash and Procure-to-Pay)

---

#### Solution: Customer Tax Classification via Business Partner Link

An approach to determine the customer tax classification by linking vendor and customer master data via business partner relationships and retrieving tax classification from the customer master (KNVI).

**Tags:** SAP, Materials Management, Taxation, Business Partner, Customization

**Prerequisites:** Business Partner (in SAP S/4HANA), KNVI

---

#### Pricing Routine

A piece of code in SAP controlling access requirements and conditions in pricing and tax determination, which can be activated and modified via Transaction VOFM.

**Also known as:** Access Requirement Routine

**Tags:** SAP, Pricing, Tax Determination, Enhancement

**Prerequisites:** Routine Activation (RV80GHEN), SAP Transaction VOFM

**Related:** Routine 8 (Export Business), Routine 7 (Domestic Business)

---

#### Billing Document

A document in SAP created in the billing process that finalizes pricing, indirect tax determination, and triggers accounting document creation.

**Also known as:** Invoice Document

**Tags:** SAP, Billing, Financial Accounting, Sales and Distribution

**Prerequisites:** USEREXIT_NUMBER_RANGE, USEREXIT_PRICING_PREPARE_TKOMK

---

#### Tax Code on Purchase Order

The tax code assigned at the item level in a purchase order in SAP, which determines the indirect tax and can be manually changed after being suggested or carried over.

**Tags:** SAP, taxation, purchasing

**Prerequisites:** Transaction ME21N, Purchase Order Reference

**Related:** Purchase Info Records

---

#### Purchase Info Records

SAP master data linking suppliers and materials to tax codes and other purchasing-related details, good for repeated purchases where products are supplied from the same location.

**Also known as:** Info Record, Purchasing Info Record

**Tags:** SAP, purchasing, master data, tax determination, SAP S/4HANA, procurement

**Prerequisites:** Transaction ME12, Transaction ME11

**Related:** Vendor, Tax Code on Purchase Order

---

#### Extension of Standard Access Sequences

Modifications or additions to standard SAP access sequences for indirect tax determination to accommodate specific regional requirements that are not covered by standard settings, such as inclusion of special regions.

**Prerequisites:** Special Regions in Tax Determination, Standard Access Sequence 0003

A specification assigned to condition tables that restricts their applicability to certain business scenarios such as export or domestic business in SAP pricing.

**Also known as:** Requirement

**Tags:** SAP, Pricing, Condition Table

**Prerequisites:** Standard Condition Table A002, Standard Condition Table A011

---

#### Pricing Date Determination

The method in SAP (adjusted at condition type level via transaction V/06) that selects the date used for tax and rebate conditions, often prioritizing invoice date, otherwise document creation date.

**Prerequisites:** Pricing Date Rules, Transaction V/06

**Related:** Billing Date (FKDAT), Billing Document Tax ID Number Transfer, Created-on Date (ERDAT)

---

#### Tax Code Properties

Settings in SAP for each tax code that include descriptions, tax type, control flags like CheckID, and EU Code indicators, defining the behavior and reporting attributes of the tax code.

**Also known as:** Tax Code Settings

**Tags:** Financial Accounting, SAP Configuration, Taxation

**Prerequisites:** EU Code (Tax Code), Tax Type

---

#### Fixed Remuneration Model

A standardized approach under Amount B that sets predetermined, safe-harbor-based payments for baseline marketing and sales activities to minimize tax disputes.

**Tags:** Transfer Pricing, Tax Compliance

**Prerequisites:** Risk Assessment Approach, Marketing and Sales Functions

**Related:** Safe Harbor

---

#### Periodicity of Report Category

The defined schedule and time parameters that specify how often and within what period a report category must generate reporting tasks in SAP.

**Prerequisites:** Active-From Date and Active-To Date, Fiscal Year Variant (FY Variant)

**Related:** Report Categories

---

#### Opting for Taxation

A legal choice allowing a taxable person to waive a tax exemption, thereby subjecting transactions to tax and enabling input tax deduction.

**Also known as:** Tax Option

**Tags:** tax policy, indirect taxation, tax administration

**Prerequisites:** EU VAT Directive (Article 137), Taxable Transaction

---

#### Tax-Relevant Data

Data recorded during business processes that impacts various types of taxes, including business partner information, material types, procurement details, and pricing conditions.

**Also known as:** Tax Data

**Tags:** taxation, data management, tax management, data

**Prerequisites:** Procure-to-Pay Scenario, Enterprise resource planning (ERP) tax determination

---

#### Branch Model

A corporate structure where branches act as low-risk distributors under a buy-sell arrangement to centralize tax management and avoid high margins in foreign branches.

**Tags:** corporate structure, tax planning

**Prerequisites:** Low-Risk Distributor, Buy-Sell Structure

---

#### End-to-End Scenarios

Comprehensive descriptions of all process steps within business processes that are practically applicable in ERP and IT landscapes, potentially modified by industry-specific characteristics.

**Tags:** business processes, ERP, process management

**Prerequisites:** Business Processes, SAP S/4HANA Transformations

---

#### Pricing Date (Sales)

The date used in sales documents to determine the exchange rate to apply; typically based on the requested delivery date but can be configured differently depending on document type or conditions.

**Tags:** Sales and Distribution, Pricing, Currency Exchange

**Prerequisites:** Sales Document Types (Pricing Date), Condition Type (Pricing Date Setting)

**Related:** Pricing Exchange Rate Type

---

#### Nondeductible VAT Account

An account used to record nondeductible input VAT amounts in SAP, which cannot be recovered and are either allocated to expenses or posted as separate line items depending on account keys.

**Tags:** SAP, Financial Accounting, VAT, General Ledger

**Prerequisites:** Account Key NAV, Account Key NVV

**Related:** Input VAT Account

---

#### Indirect Tax Rate

A specific percentage rate applied to taxable transactions that determines the amount of indirect tax due, commonly assigned one-per-tax code in SAP.

**Also known as:** Tax Rate

**Tags:** SAP, Indirect Tax, Tax Codes

**Prerequisites:** Time-Dependent Taxes, Tax Category

**Related:** Jurisdiction Indicators

---

#### Company Code Country

The country assigned to a company code in SAP which triggers the use of the corresponding tax procedure for indirect tax calculation.

**Prerequisites:** Company Code (SAP), Country-Specific Withholding Tax Settings

**Related:** Tax Category

Programming enhancements using ABAP or user exits in SAP S/4HANA to tailor tax determination logic beyond standard capabilities.

A sales order in SAP containing an item that triggers the third-party procurement process, using a special item category to initiate purchase requisitions and vendor deliveries.

An entity such as a customer or vendor registered in SAP with tax-relevant information used for processing direct taxes.

#### Standard Condition Tables (A002, A011, A080, A086, A087)

Predefined SAP condition tables used for indirect tax and pricing purposes covering different combinations of tax indicators such as material, plant, import, region, and account assignment.

---

#### Time-Dependent Tax Codes

Tax codes in SAP with validity periods allowing automatic adjustment of tax rates over time, introduced in SAP S/4HANA Cloud release 2202.

**Tags:** SAP, Taxation, Time Dependency

**Prerequisites:** Country/Region Solution Activation, Migration of Custom and Transactional Data

**Related:** Retroactive Tax Rate Maintenance

#### Implementation (of tax determination)

The effort and process involved in setting up tax determination rules and logic in SAP, either natively, via add-ons, or through external engines.

**Related:** Scalability (in tax determination), Pricing of Tax Solutions

---

#### EU Countries

Countries that are members of the European Union, which have specific tax rules affecting indirect tax determinations and access requirements.

**Also known as:** European Union Member States

**Tags:** geography, taxation, indirect tax, regulation

**Prerequisites:** Access Requirement 8, Access Requirement 7, XEGLD Indicator

---

#### Tax Group

A legal arrangement where two or more entities under common control or economic integration are treated as a single taxable entity for indirect tax purposes.

**Also known as:** VAT Group, Taxable Group

**Tags:** indirect taxation, tax compliance, corporate structure, tax, classification, reporting

**Prerequisites:** Financial Link (in Tax Grouping), Economic Link (in Tax Grouping), Organizational Link (in Tax Grouping)

**Related:** Mandatory Grouping, Voluntary Grouping

---

#### Condition Type TTX1

A condition type used in SAP pricing procedures that relates to tax categorization and determines indirect tax calculation based on material and country-specific tax classifications.

**Also known as:** Tax Condition Type TTX1

**Tags:** SAP Pricing, Taxation

**Prerequisites:** Requirement (Condition Requirement), Tax Indicator for Material in Materials Management, Material Tax Classification in Sales and Distribution

**Related:** Alternative Calculation Routine for Condition Amount, Alternative Calculation Routine for Condition Base Value

---

#### Tax Indicator for Account Assignment

A classification used in SAP to indicate whether a procurement item affected via a specific auxiliary account is subject to indirect tax, enabling tax calculation based on purchase reason.

**Also known as:** Account Assignment Tax Indicator

**Tags:** SAP, purchasing, indirect tax, tax configuration

**Prerequisites:** Auxiliary Account, Transaction OMKL, Transaction OMKO

**Related:** Standard Product

---

#### Tax Data Management

The process and systems used to collect, manage, and prepare tax-related data for reporting and compliance purposes, acting as an intermediary between ERP systems and tax reporting solutions.

**Also known as:** Tax data processing

**Tags:** Data Management, Taxation, Information Systems, Tax Compliance, tax, tax data, IT enablement, SAP S/4HANA, business process

**Prerequisites:** Data Sources, Data Provider, Interface Requirements, Manual Data Maintenance

---

#### Indirect Tax Determination

The process of establishing applicable indirect taxes such as VAT during sales transactions, influenced by factors like transport responsibility, residence of involved parties, and transaction type.

**Also known as:** Sales and Purchase Tax Calculation, Tax Determination, Indirect Tax Calculation, VAT Determination, Tax Calculation, Sales Tax Determination

**Tags:** taxation, SAP, sales and purchasing, Indirect Tax, Pricing, Sales Distribution, automation, Compliance, SAP S/4HANA, financial accounting, Sales and Distribution, finance, sales, distribution, tax, accounting, tax determination

**Prerequisites:** SAP Table T005, Customer Master Data Field KNA1-STCEG, Purchase Info Records (EINA and EINE), Ship-to Address, Special territories in tax determination

**Related:** Middleman VAT Responsibility Rule (EU rule since 2020), Distribution channel, Dispatch

#### Transfer Pricing Testing

---

Mode of transport code categorizes the means by which goods cross borders within the EU and is used in Intrastat reporting to specify transportation method at borders.

**Prerequisites:** Intrastat report

**Related:** Sea transport, Air transport, Rail transport, Road transport, Stationary modes of transport

---

Movement code is a specific classification used in some countries’ Intrastat reporting to indicate the type or nature of goods movement, such as in the Czech Republic.

**Also known as:** Intrastat movement code

**Tags:** Intrastat, Trade classification, Country-specific codes

**Prerequisites:** Intrastat report

---

---

**Prerequisites:** Custom Fields

---

The ABAP Dictionary tool in SAP used to define and manage database tables, views, and append structures including creation of custom fields.

**Tags:** SAP S/4HANA, Development, Extensibility, SAP, Transaction Code, Data Modeling, Configuration

A VAT ID determination rule where only the VAT ID of the payer in the tax destination country is considered for tax purposes, ignoring VAT IDs from sold-to or ship-to parties.

**Also known as:** Rule B (tax destination country)

**Tags:** tax rules, SAP configuration

**Prerequisites:** tax determination rules

A VAT ID determination rule allowing the payer's VAT ID from their country of residence to be considered if no VAT ID exists for the tax destination country, deviating from SAP standard behavior.

### Level 3 - Advanced

#### Transfer Pricing Reporting

SAP S/4HANA functionalities and additional reporting capabilities aimed at providing insights into transfer pricing risks, provisions, margins, and key performance indicators relevant for regulatory compliance.

**Prerequisites:** Transfer Pricing Testing

#### Balance carryforward

Balance carryforward is the process of transferring ledger balances from a closed posting period or fiscal year to the new period, performed independently per ledger based on its posting period variant settings.

**Also known as:** Carried forward balance, Balance Forward, Balance Rollforward

**Tags:** accounting, finance, ledger, SAP S/4HANA, Financial Accounting, Posting Period Management

**Prerequisites:** Posting period variant

---

#### Define Mode of Transport at the Border (SAP Intrastat)

Define Mode of Transport at the Border is an SAP configuration function used to set transport mode codes that describe how goods cross EU borders in Intrastat reporting.

**Also known as:** Transport mode configuration

**Tags:** SAP, Intrastat, Configuration

**Prerequisites:** Mode of transport code

---

#### Define Movement Code (SAP Intrastat)

Define Movement Code is an SAP configuration activity where movement codes are set up for specific countries to classify goods movements in Intrastat reporting.

**Also known as:** Movement Code configuration

**Tags:** SAP, Intrastat, Configuration

**Prerequisites:** Movement code

**Related:** Transaction SM30

---

#### FIN_ACDOC_EXT_SUBSTITUTION

A BAdI available since SAP S/4HANA Cloud 1705 that supports substitution (derivation and validation) logic for custom fields added to journal entry items.

**Tags:** SAP S/4HANA Cloud, BAdI, Derivation, Validation, Extensibility

**Prerequisites:** Journal Entry Item Business Context

---

#### BAdI_FINS_ACDOC_POSTING_EVENTS

A Business Add-In (BAdI) in SAP S/4HANA used for derivation of custom fields in journal entry items during financial document posting, available since SAP S/4HANA 1511.

**Tags:** SAP S/4HANA, BAdI, Extensibility, Derivation

**Prerequisites:** Journal Entry Item Business Context

---

#### Multiple Transfer Pricing Methods Application

The practice of applying different transfer pricing methods to various types of intercompany transactions such as tangible goods, services, licenses, and loans within a multinational group.

**Tags:** transfer pricing, multinational management

**Prerequisites:** Transfer Pricing Methods

---

#### Price Setting

The process of calculating and determining transfer prices based on established transfer pricing methods and policies.

**Tags:** transfer pricing, pricing strategy

**Prerequisites:** Transfer Pricing Methods

**Related:** End-to-End Transfer Pricing Process, Product Hierarchy

---

#### Condition category

Groups condition types into functional categories like tax, input tax non-deductible, or discounts for organizational and processing purposes.

**Also known as:** Cond. category

**Tags:** SAP, Pricing, Tax, Condition Types

**Prerequisites:** Condition class

---

#### Tax Code Availability and Structure

The characteristics and limitations of tax codes in SAP, including a two-character structure and time-dependent functionality, with engines offering more flexibility.

**Tags:** SAP, tax codes, configuration

**Prerequisites:** Indirect Tax Content and Maintenance

**Related:** Tax Classifications

---

#### Tax Returns and Declarations

Official reports submitted to tax authorities, prepared based on financial data, summarizing the amount of tax owed or refunded.

**Also known as:** Tax Filings

**Tags:** tax compliance, reporting

**Prerequisites:** Financial Data

---

#### Compliance Risk Analysis

The process of analyzing monitoring results to identify exceptions and assess deviations that may indicate non-compliance with tax regulations.

**Also known as:** Tax Risk Analysis

**Tags:** risk analysis, tax compliance

**Prerequisites:** Queries (in Tax Monitoring)

**Related:** Monitoring Level

---

#### Tax Tab (in Transaction MIRO)

A section within Transaction MIRO that displays tax-related information, copying tax codes from purchase orders and calculating tax amounts on incoming invoices.

**Tags:** SAP, transaction, tax determination

**Prerequisites:** Tax Number Validation

---

#### Amount A

A mechanism under Pillar One that allocates additional taxing rights to market jurisdictions via a formulaic apportionment of a multinational enterprise's residual nonroutine profits.

**Tags:** International Taxation, Tax Reform, Corporate Taxation

**Prerequisites:** Profit Allocation Rules

**Related:** Pillar One

---

#### Advanced Compliance Reporting (ACR)

An enhanced SAP compliance reporting solution requiring an additional license, providing advanced features for managing country-specific tax reporting requirements within SAP S/4HANA.

**Also known as:** SAP ACR

**Tags:** SAP, Tax Reporting, Compliance, S/4HANA, Advanced Reporting

**Prerequisites:** Basic Compliance Reporting

**Related:** SAP S/4HANA Tax Reporting

---

#### Run Phase

The SAP Activate phase marking the end of implementation and the beginning of system operation and ongoing maintenance.

**Tags:** SAP Activate, operation, maintenance

**Prerequisites:** Go-live

---

#### Realize Phase

The SAP Activate phase involving development, testing, tax determination setup, master data management, reporting configuration, and quality assurance.

**Tags:** SAP Activate, project phase, development

**Prerequisites:** Explore Phase

**Related:** SAP Activate Methodology Phases, Tax-compliant Setup, V-model

---

#### Control Data Tab

A tab within the G/L account master record in SAP where tax-relevant settings such as the Tax Category are maintained.

**Tags:** SAP, Financial Accounting, G/L Account Master Data, User Interface

**Prerequisites:** Tax Category

---

#### Assigning Tax Procedures to Countries

The process of linking a tax procedure to a country's settings in SAP Financial Accounting to enable correct indirect tax calculation for that jurisdiction.

**Tags:** SAP Configuration, Indirect Tax, Country Setup

**Prerequisites:** Preassigned Tax Procedures

---

#### Tax Treatment

The specific manner in which tax is applied to a business transaction, determining whether and how tax is calculated and reported.

**Tags:** Taxation, Accounting

**Prerequisites:** Tax Base Amount

**Related:** Transaction Type (Tax)

---

#### ERP Software Implementation Project

A comprehensive organizational project to deploy or upgrade ERP software, integrating business processes and improving operational efficiency, including tax integration opportunities.

**Also known as:** ERP Implementation

**Tags:** ERP, project management, business transformation

**Prerequisites:** SAP Activate Methodology

---

#### Time-dependent tax calculation

A tax calculation approach that considers temporal factors such as changing tax rates, valid periods, or effective dates influencing the tax computation.

**Also known as:** Temporal tax calculation

**Tags:** tax calculation, time-based taxation

**Prerequisites:** Tax calculation

---

#### Billing Quantity

A quantity used for billing calculation which follows rules based on whether the invoice is sales order-based (Rule A) or delivery-based (Rule B), reflecting uninvoiced order or delivery quantities.

**Tags:** billing, quantity, sales order, delivery

**Prerequisites:** Copying Control

**Related:** VF Transactions, Pos./Neg. Quantity

---

#### VAT ID Determination Rules (SAP)

A set of configurable rules in SAP that define which business partner role's VAT ID to use for tax determination, including several predefined rules (blank, A, B, C, D).

**Also known as:** VAT ID Rules, VAT ID Determination Logic

**Tags:** SAP, VAT, tax determination

**Prerequisites:** Value-Added Tax (VAT) ID Determination

**Related:** Rule D for VAT ID Determination, Rule C for VAT ID Determination, Rule B for VAT ID Determination, Rule A for VAT ID Determination, Rule <blank> for VAT ID Determination

---

#### Condition Table A088

A purchasing condition table in SAP materials management that includes taxes based on material, plant, account assignment, origin, and region for indirect tax determination.

**Also known as:** Table A088 (Taxes: Material, Plant, Acc. Assignment, Origin and Region)

**Tags:** SAP, Purchasing, Materials Management, Condition Table

**Prerequisites:** Tax Code Determination, Tax Classifications and Indicators

**Related:** Standard Access Sequence 0003

---

#### Tax Compliance Management System

A system designed to operationalize and automate compliance with tax regulations to mitigate tax-related risks such as incorrect invoices, misreported taxes, penalties, and customs issues.

**Also known as:** Tax compliance system

**Tags:** Tax Compliance, Risk Management, Operational Systems

**Prerequisites:** IT Infrastructure and Architecture (Tax), Operational Tax Data Management

**Related:** Digitization (in Tax), Tax Processes, Electronic Document Processing

---

#### Tax Determination with Pricing

The process in SAP where tax calculations are integrated within the pricing procedure to compute applicable taxes during sales and purchasing.

**Tags:** SAP S/4HANA, tax calculation, pricing

**Prerequisites:** Integration of SAP Modules in Tax Determination, Price Calculation Procedure, Customization Scenarios

**Related:** Solutions for Procurement

---

### Level 4 - Expert

#### Operational Transfer Prices

The practical application and execution of transfer pricing policies through processes, systems, and organizational actions to ensure compliance and management effectiveness.

**Also known as:** Operational Transfer Pricing

**Tags:** transfer pricing, operations management

**Prerequisites:** Price Setting

**Related:** End-to-End Transfer Pricing Process

---

#### Corrective Actions

Steps taken to address deviations or exceptions found during monitoring to bring tax compliance processes back into conformity.

**Also known as:** Remediation Actions

**Tags:** tax compliance, process correction

**Prerequisites:** Compliance Risk Analysis

**Related:** Monitoring Level

---

### Level 5 - Specialized

#### Monitoring and Adjusting

Ongoing activities in transfer pricing to ensure the pricing policy is properly executed and adjusted to maintain compliance and optimize performance.

**Tags:** transfer pricing, compliance, management

**Prerequisites:** Operational Transfer Prices

**Related:** End-to-End Transfer Pricing Process

---

#### Correction Tasks

Follow-up activities to ensure that corrective actions are completed and that compliance risks are resolved.

**Also known as:** Correction Follow-up

**Tags:** compliance management, remediation

**Prerequisites:** Corrective Actions

**Related:** Worklists (Tax Compliance)

---
